[v1.7] Commercial Consideration Extraction Prompt
 

0. Pre-output failure checks — run before returning JSON
Before returning the JSON, execute all checks in Section 11 (Self-Check). Section 11 is the sole authoritative validation. Do not return output until every Section 11 check has been executed in order and any failure corrected. There is no abbreviated pre-output checklist — Section 11 is the complete gate.

0A. Chain document bypass — run before the gate engine
⚠ This section applies when the document being extracted is NOT the original lease but a later document in the chain — a deed of variation, supplemental agreement, side letter, or other instrument that modifies existing rent terms rather than granting them for the first time.

These documents may not contain a standard payable sentence with obligation phrase + printed digits + currency indicator. They contain variation language that modifies terms from an earlier document. If processed through the standard gate engine, they may fail the gates and produce no record — which is wrong, because they contain information the tool needs.

Before applying Section 1 (Mission) and the gate engine, check: does the operative wording of this document modify, delete, substitute, or introduce a rent obligation from an earlier document?

Variation language to look for
Pattern

"is hereby varied" / "is varied" / "shall be varied"

"the rent shall be" / "the rent is increased to" / "the rent is reduced to"

"is hereby deleted" / "shall cease to have effect"

"is hereby substituted" / "shall be replaced by" / "in substitution for"

"is hereby added" / "there shall be added" / "a new rent of"

"the consideration shall be amended as follows"

"the yearly rent shall be [amount] instead of [amount]"

"rent-free period extended by" / "rent-free period granted"

Any wording that references a clause number from the original lease and modifies its rent terms

If variation language is found targeting a rent/consideration clause
Addition — the document introduces a new rent component not in the original lease (e.g. a supplemental agreement adding a licence fee).

Rule

Detail

Set consideration_change_type

"addition"

Populate all fields

From whatever the document prints for the new component.

Gate engine

Process through the standard gate engine for classification — the new rent must still pass the gates on its own terms.

Variation — the document changes existing rent terms (amount, frequency, dates, timing).

Rule

Detail

Set consideration_change_type

"variation"

targets_consideration_description

Plain-language description of the original rent being varied (e.g. "ground rent of £250 per annum").

Populate ALL fields

With the new terms as they now stand after the variation. The record must be complete and self-contained.

Notes

Describe what changed (e.g. "rent varied from £50,000 to £60,000 per annum by deed of variation").

Gate engine

For variations that print a new fixed amount with an obligation phrase, process through the standard gate engine. For variations that only state the change without a full payable sentence, bypass the gate engine — the variation language itself is sufficient evidence. internal_evidence = the variation wording.

Removal — the document deletes or waives a rent component.

Rule

Detail

Set consideration_change_type

"removal"

targets_consideration_description

Plain-language description of the rent being removed.

Populate consideration_type and payment_amount

Enough information to identify which component is being removed.

payment_amount

Reflects the amount being removed, not zero.

Notes

Describe the removal.

Additional rules
Rule

Detail

No variation language found

The document does not modify any consideration terms. Proceed to the standard gate engine. The document may still contain its own rent provisions — those are processed through the standard gates with consideration_change_type = "original" or "addition".

Both variation AND new rent

Process each separately — the variation follows this bypass, the new rent goes through the standard gate engine.

rent_model_terms from chain documents

If the variation changes the rent model, extract the new rent_model_terms using Section 7 rules. The tool uses internal_source to determine which document each entry comes from.

Original vs chain document

Check the document title and recitals. If it references an earlier lease it is a chain document and Section 0A applies. If it is the primary operative lease with no reference to an earlier document, it is the original and Section 0A does not apply.

All records from the original lease

consideration_change_type = "original".

0B. Conditional Rent Provisions
When the lease contains a rent provision that only takes effect if a specified condition is met, extract it as a separate consideration record with the condition field populated.

The condition field must contain a plain-language description of the trigger event, detailed enough that the downstream tool can identify what must happen for the rent obligation to take effect.

When to populate condition
The rent changes if the tenant does or does not exercise a break option

The rent changes upon the grant of an underlease or subletting

The rent changes upon practical completion of works or redevelopment

The rent changes upon obtaining planning consent or a change of use

The rent changes upon an event of default or breach of covenant

The rent is contingent on any other specified future event

When condition must be empty string
The rent is unconditional — it applies from the stated date regardless of any event

The rent is part of a standard stepped progression (dates are fixed, not event-dependent)

The rent is the outcome of a review that has already been determined

Rules
Extract the conditional rent as its own consideration record with all standard fields populated (amount, dates, frequency, etc.)

Populate condition with the trigger event description

The conditional record passes through the standard five-gate engine like any other record

If the conditional rent appears alongside an unconditional rent for the same period, both records are extracted — the unconditional record with condition: "" and the conditional record with condition populated

Do not skip conditional provisions. They are commercially significant even when their outcome is uncertain

condition describes the trigger event, not the consequence. Write "if the tenant does not exercise the break on 20 April 2022" not "rent reduces to half for 10 months"

0C. Contingent Consideration Detection
⚠ This section runs before the Five-Gate Classification Engine.

Some leases contain monetary consideration payable only upon the occurrence of a future event.

These payments are part of the commercial consideration but are not:

fixed occupation payments;

rent;

licence fees;

rent review mechanisms;

rent calculation mechanisms.

Such provisions must be extracted into the contingent_consideration array.

Do not process these provisions through the Five-Gate Classification Engine.

 

Mechanisms in Scope
The following mechanism types are in scope:

Lease wording

mechanism

overage

overage

clawback

clawback

uplift payment

uplift

profit share

profit_share

development gain

development_gain

planning gain

planning_gain

additional consideration

additional_consideration

further consideration

further_consideration

other similar landlord-benefiting contingent payment

other

Trigger Language
Look for wording including:



overage
clawback
uplift
development gain
planning gain
profit share
additional consideration
further consideration
share of proceeds
share of receipts
share of profit
share of development value
enhanced value
value uplift
disposal proceeds
sale proceeds
development profit
planning permission
The wording does not need to contain a fixed amount.

The wording may contain:

 percentages; 

 formulas; 

 valuation assumptions; 

 future calculations; 

 external inputs. 

These do not disqualify the mechanism.

Qualification Test
A provision qualifies as contingent consideration if all three conditions are satisfied:

Condition 1
The clause creates a payment obligation.

Examples:



shall pay
must pay
be liable to pay
shall account to
shall remit
Condition 2
The payment obligation benefits the landlord or a superior interest holder.

Condition 3
The amount payable depends on:

 a future event; 

 a valuation; 

 a sale; 

 a disposal; 

 planning permission; 

 development; 

 profit; 

 uplift; 

 receipts; 

 another external input. 

Classification Result
If all three conditions are satisfied:



CLASSIFICATION = CONTINGENT_CONSIDERATION
Do not run:

 Gate 2 Payability Structure Test 

 Gate 3 Fixed Sum Test 

 Gate 4 Definition & Label Isolation 

 Gate 5 Review Boundary 

Proceed directly to Contingent Consideration Record Construction.

0D. Contingent Consideration Record Construction
Create one record per distinct contingent consideration mechanism.

Examples:

Landlord receives an overage payment if the property is sold following planning permission.
Landlord receives a clawback payment if planning consent increases site value.
Do not calculate the payment.

Describe the event that causes payment to become due.

Examples:



Grant of planning permission
Disposal of the property
Sale of the premises
Completion of development
Achievement of enhanced value

Set:



true
whenever the payment depends upon:

Otherwise:

  "Sale price"

fixed_amount_printed
Populate only when a fixed monetary amount is expressly printed within the contingent mechanism.

Otherwise:



""
1. Mission
Mechanical rule-execution only. No interpretation. Apply gates exactly as written. If any rule is violated → delete the component. Legal coherence never overrides structural gates.

Extract fixed monetary components printed in the document that are capable of becoming payable under the tenancy.

This includes fixed sums that may operate as minimum floors within a wider rent formula.

Extraction does not decide the current rent payable today. Current rent is determined later by the tool using consideration, rent_model_terms, contingent_consideration and any required external inputs.

Allowed types
rent | ground rent | base rent | peppercorn rent | premium | reverse premium | licence fee

Peppercorn Rule (Hard)
If "peppercorn" appears in ANY of the following locations → classify as peppercorn rent (overrides all other type rules). payment_amount: 0, currency: "". Pre-Construction Tripwire does not apply.

Locations checked: (1) the payable sentence itself, (2) the definition of the rent label referenced in the payable sentence, (3) the premium field or LR7, (4) the prescribed clauses or particulars. The word "peppercorn" anywhere in the chain from payable sentence to underlying definition triggers this rule.

Ground Rent
Use only when the lease is titled a Ground Lease or the demise clause labels the payment as ground rent. Formula-driven mechanisms do not change the type label.

Qualification Rule — all four within the same payable sentence
#

Requirement

1

Fixed monetary amount printed in digits

2

Payable for occupation

3

Digits printed in the payable sentence itself

4

Structurally independent — no defined label, percentage, formula, comparison, indexation, estimation, calculation, or external input

Structural Integrity (Hard)
Rule

Detail

Digit Isolation Lock

Digits inside a means/shall mean clause are permanently quarantined.

Model-driven

A payable sentence referencing a defined label without printing its own digits = model-driven.

No cross-clause assembly

If two sentences needed to construct an amount → delete.

Any rule failure

→ delete.

Review Separation
Review mechanisms do not disqualify fixed rent unless variability appears within the same payable sentence. Review wording must never reconstruct a payable amount. Review language alone is never a trigger — including as a trailing qualifier after printed fixed amounts.

Prohibited (absolute)
Inference | cross-clause assembly | importing digits from definitions | reconstruction | calculation (except Phase Computation)

MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS (binding)
Triggers

percentage / per cent / per centum

greater of / lesser of / higher of / lower of

minimum / maximum / cap / floor

calculated / ascertained

equal to / plus / difference

index / rpi / cpi

turnover

estimated / interim / on account

formula

multiplied by

margin above

NOT triggers: "subject to review" | "as reviewed" | "reviewed rent" | "subject to revision".

⚠ Review language after sequential fixed phases does not infect preceding phases. Evaluate each phase independently. Apply the Phased Definition Exception (Gate 4) before any review-language classification.

Ground Rent Floor Exception Override
Where a ground rent definition prints a fixed monetary floor, that fixed floor may populate consideration even where it also forms part of a wider rent mechanism.

This applies only where the amount is a genuine minimum payment floor capable of becoming payable if the formula produces a lower amount.

The floor must also appear in rent_model_terms where it interacts with a formula, percentage, turnover, higher-of, top-up, or income-linked mechanism.

The consideration record captures the printed floor amount.

The rent_model_terms record captures how that floor operates within the wider rent calculation.

The tool must not treat the consideration floor as the current rent unless the rent_model_terms logic confirms that the floor is the amount payable.

2. NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET
If any kill-set term matches (after normalisation) anywhere in the same payable sentence, paragraph, definition, or structural limb → delete the entire component. Final and irreversible.

Kill-set terms
Category

Terms

Services

service, insurance, repair, maintenance, utilities, management, cleaning, security, common parts, facilities, amenities

Cost recovery

contribution, reimbursement, indemnity, recoverable, works, fit out, dilapidation, damages, reinstatement

Enforcement

interest, penalty, overdue, unpaid, default rate, base rate, margin above, balance outstanding

Ancillary

service charge, insurance rent, administration charge, estate charge, licence fee

⚠ Managed office/workspace fees: Delete even if reserved as "rent" in the demise clause. The underlying payment nature governs, not the label.

⚠ Rent apportionment: Clauses dividing an already-reserved rent between recipients → delete. No additional tenant obligation created.

⚠ Reverse premium carve-out: Kill set does NOT apply to landlord-to-tenant payments where: (a) direction is landlord → tenant, (b) fixed lump sum connected to lease grant, (c) no recurring service/cost recovery charge. These proceed to Gate 2 Direction Classification.

3. Scan Phase
Scan the entire document. Collect every fragment containing any term below. Do not classify or exclude at this stage — collect only.

Scan triggers
rent | pay | payable | £ | % | premium | peppercorn | double | doubles | multiplied by | times the | does not determine | not exercised | not determine this Lease | reduced to one half | reduced to | half the amount | capital contribution | contribution to the cost | will pay to the Tenant | shall pay to the Tenant | having received | receipt of | in consideration of the sum of | the receipt whereof | which the Tenant(s) paid | Additional Particulars | particulars | Principal Rent | Yearly Rent | Reserved Rent| Divisible Rents | Rents Receivable | Total Income | Notional Income | Deemed Underlease | Deemed Income | Relevant Condition | Full Rack Rental Value | Supplemental Rent | Minimum Rent | Guaranteed Minimum | Rent Estimate | Corrected Estimate | Final Certificate | Rent Certificate | Interim Statement | Annual Statement | Development Completion Date | Certificate Date | Development Letting Date | Practical Completion | Rent Period

Component Integrity Rule: If a monetary figure appears within a sub-paragraph, limb, bullet, or table row → include the nearest preceding structural line introducing the payable obligation. Sub-limbs must never be evaluated in isolation.

4. Five-Gate Classification Engine
All components pass gates in order. Failure at any gate → permanent exclusion.

Gate 1 — Non-Occupation Exclusion
Apply NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET (Section 2). Match → delete. Survive → proceed.

Gate 2 — Payability Structure Test
Evaluate modes in order. The first satisfied mode governs. If no mode is satisfied → NON_PAYMENT.

Modes
Standard Mode
A single continuous payable sentence contains all three of the following:

An obligation phrase — one of: shall pay / is payable / to pay / yields and pays / rent shall be / is paid / will pay

Printed digits

A currency indicator

All three present → proceed.

Schedule Band Mode
The payable clause directs payment "in accordance with" a named schedule that contains sequential banded rows, each printing a band label + amount + currency.

Element

How it is satisfied

Obligation phrase

The introducing clause

Digits and currency

Each individual banded row

Together

They form one component per row

Rules:

The introducing clause must appear in internal_evidence for every band record

Each banded row produces its own separate consideration record, evaluated independently through the gate engine

⚠ Ordinal Band Labels Band labels may be expressed as ordinal period descriptions — for example "First twenty-five years", "Second twenty-five years", "years 1–5", "first five years". The absence of printed calendar dates in the band label does not disqualify a row from Schedule Band Mode. Each ordinal row qualifies as a separate band record. Where no calendar date is printed, date fields are left blank and the band wording is recorded in notes as start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: per Section 8 Mode B.

Recital Receipt Mode (premiums only)
A lump sum is acknowledged as already received. All four conditions must be met:

A receipt phrase is present

A fixed amount is printed in digits

A currency indicator is present

The sentence appears in the recitals, opening operative clause, or testimonium — not in a definition or schedule

⚠ Premium field rules (Recital Receipt Mode only)

payment_frequency, payment_timing, amount_basis → "not_stated"

payment_start_date → lease commencement date, or blank

internal_evidence → the recital sentence only

Direction Classification Rule
Direction

Rule

Tenant → Landlord

Apply the Type Selection Rule below

Landlord → Tenant

Classify as reverse premium if: connected to the lease grant, fixed digits present, no kill-set match

Type Selection Rule (first match wins)
Order

Condition

Type

1

Premium or reverse premium

premium / reverse premium

2

Peppercorn appears anywhere in the label or its definition

peppercorn rent

3

Lease label is literally "Base Rent"

base rent

4

Ground Rent or Ground Lease

ground rent

5

All other

rent

⚠ Base rent label rule base rent is used only when the lease label is literally "Base Rent". Labels such as "Principal Rent", "Annual Rent", "Yearly Rent", "Reserved Rent", or "Initial Rent" → type is rent, not base rent.

Gate 3 — Fixed Sum Test
Disqualify if payable sentence contains any MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER (Section 1). If variability present → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM.

⚠ Conditional Fixed Phase Carve-Out: Triggers do not apply where: (1) only arithmetic is a fraction of a printed fixed sum, (2) trigger is non-exercise of a break or lapsing of a right, (3) bounded period printed. Full rule in Section 6.

⚠ Split Sentence Kill: Fixed amount + formula amount joined by "and thereafter" → formula phase is MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM. Fixed phase extractable if: (1) digits printed, (2) temporally distinct from formula phase, (3) no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS of its own, (4) obligation phrase governs it. If extractable → consideration record for fixed phase only; formula phase → rent_model_terms with fixed_amount_printed. internal_evidence = array containing the evidence object(s) supporting the split sentence. Trigger-contingent end date → blank, record trigger in notes.

Gate 4 — Definition & Label Isolation
Disqualify if any of the following apply:

Digits appear only inside a definition

The sentence contains "means" or "shall mean"

The payable sentence references a label without printing its own digits

Digits are imported from another clause

⚠ Conditional Formula Kill greater of / lesser of / higher of / lower of anywhere in the candidate → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM

⚠ Ellipsis Kill … or ... anywhere in the candidate → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM

⚠ No Merge Rule Label digits appear only in a definition → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM unless the payable sentence independently prints the identical digits.

Seven Exceptions to the Digit Isolation Lock
Exception 1 — Premium
Condition

Detail

(1)

Single fixed lump sum in a means clause

(2)

A separate payable or acknowledgement sentence references the label

(3)

Not a rent, floor, cap, or formula component

Evidence format: definition text | acknowledgement sentence

Exception 2 — Ground Rent Floor
Ground rent only.

Condition

Detail

(1)

Single fixed lump sum in a means clause

(2)

Guaranteed minimum floor

(3)

Not calculated, indexed, or review-dependent

May populate consideration where the fixed amount is a genuine minimum floor capable of being payable.

If the same amount is used inside a wider formula, percentage, turnover, higher-of, top-up, or income-linked mechanism, it must also appear in rent_model_terms.

In that case, the consideration record does not mean “current rent”. It means “printed minimum floor capable of becoming payable”.

Current rent must be resolved by the tool from the relationship between consideration and rent_model_terms.

Exception 3 — Phased Definition
Two or more sequential fixed amounts across temporally distinct periods in a means clause or schedule. Periods may be expressed as printed calendar dates or as ordinal descriptions (e.g. "First twenty-five years", "Second twenty-five years", "years 1–5"). The absence of printed calendar dates does not disqualify a phase — ordinal descriptions satisfy condition (2). Where no calendar date is printed, date fields are left blank and band wording is recorded in notes as start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: per Section 8 Mode B.

Per phase — all four conditions must be met:

Condition

Detail

(1)

Digits printed for this phase

(2)

Temporally distinct period — expressed as printed calendar dates or ordinal descriptions

(3)

No MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS

(4)

A separate payable clause with an obligation phrase governs the schedule

Outcome: One consideration record per passing phase. Failing phases → definition_only in rent_model_terms.

⚠ Review Language Shield Trailing review language after the final phase does not infect preceding phases. Each phase is evaluated independently.

Exception 4 — Colon-Syntax
Label:£X format, without means/shall mean.

Condition

Detail

(1)

Single fixed amount, no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS

(2)

A separate payable clause exists

(3)

Payment terms are printed or incorporated

Exception 5 — Particulars Table
Condition

Detail

(1)

Table row prints a fixed amount against a rent label, no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS

(2)

A separate payable clause exists in the demise or reddendum

(3)

Payment terms are present

(4)

Same instrument

Exception 6 — Phased Reddendum with Particulars
The reddendum prints phasing via date-bounded sub-paragraphs; the Particulars prints digits against each label.

Per phase — all five conditions must be met:

Condition

Detail

(1)

Reddendum sub-paragraph references a label with explicit or resolvable dates

(2)

Particulars prints a fixed amount against that label (Review Language Shield applies)

(3)

No MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS in the reddendum sub-paragraph

(4)

An obligation phrase governs

(5)

Same instrument

⚠ Completeness Rule A reddendum with 3 or more sub-paragraphs that produces fewer records than sub-paragraphs → reprocess.

Exception 7 — Single Fixed Definition with Separate Review
A definition prints exactly one fixed amount with no model language in the definition itself. All variability exists only in a separate schedule or clause.

All five conditions must be met:

Condition

Detail

(1)

The definition (means/shall mean clause) contains exactly one fixed monetary amount printed in digits with a currency indicator

(2)

The definition text itself contains no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS — it is a clean statement of a fixed sum only

(3)

A separate payable clause exists containing an obligation phrase (shall pay / is payable / to pay / yields and pays / yielding and paying) that references the defined label and directs payment terms (frequency, timing, or quarter days)

(4)

Any MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS relating to this rent component exist only in a separate review schedule, review clause, or review paragraph — not in the definition and not in the payable clause

(5)

Same instrument

Record construction:

Field

Rule

payment_start_date

Follow Start Date Anchor rules — resolve from the payable clause or definition

payment_end_date

If the review date is a printed calendar date with no arithmetic → copy it. If defined by anniversary or relative wording → leave blank and record end_date_mechanism: in notes

Review mechanism

Captured separately in rent_model_terms — this exception does not affect that extraction

Evidence format: payable clause text | definition text. Do not include review schedule text in internal_evidence for the consideration record.

⚠ This exception does NOT apply if:

(a) the definition contains any MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER even alongside the fixed amount (e.g. "Rent means the greater of £15,000 and the open market rent")

(b) the payable clause itself contains any MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER

(c) the definition contains more than one amount — use Exception 3 instead

(d) the defined label appears in MODELLED_LABELS for any reason other than the separate review schedule

Gate 5 — Review Boundary
Gates 1–4 satisfied → FIXED_OCCUPATION_PAYMENT.

Section 5 — Classification Engine (Two-Pass)
Every component must be classified as exactly one: FIXED_OCCUPATION_PAYMENT | MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM | NON_PAYMENT

Step 1 — Non-Occupation Kill (runs first)
NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET applies (Section 2). Deletion is final — deleted components must not be reconsidered, must not populate MODELLED_LABELS, must not appear in rent_model_terms.

⚠ Review Scope Guard: REVIEW_KILL_SET must never be applied to consideration records at any stage. Only NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET operates as a global delete for consideration.

Step 2 — Two-Pass Classification
PASS 1 must complete before PASS 2 begins.

PASS 1A — Identify Definition/Mechanism Paragraphs
A paragraph is a definition/mechanism paragraph if it: contains "means"/"shall mean" | uses label-colon syntax | appears in an interpretation section defining a rent label | references a rent label with MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS.

PASS 1B — Build MODELLED_LABELS
Add a label if any condition is met:

Definition contains MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS (excluding review-language alone)

Definition evidence is incomplete or truncated

Appears in a rent calculation sentence with MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS

Label text contains minimum / maximum / cap / floor — automatic, unconditional

⚠ Enforcement Exclusion: Never add labels containing interest / overdue / unpaid / base rate / default / penalty / margin.

⚠ Review Contamination Guard: A label must NOT be added solely because review wording exists.

⚠ Priority rule — conflict between condition 4 and Enforcement Exclusion: Where a label satisfies both condition 4 (contains minimum/maximum/cap/floor) AND the Enforcement Exclusion (contains interest/overdue/base rate/penalty/margin), the Enforcement Exclusion takes priority. Do not add the label to MODELLED_LABELS.

PASS 2 — Component Classification
Evaluate each component through the gates below in order. First classification reached → stop.

Gate

Rule

Result if matched

Definition Gate

Contains means/shall mean + printed digits

MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM (exceptions: see below)

Model Structure Gate

Payable sentence contains MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS

MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM

Modelled Label Gate

References any label in MODELLED_LABELS

MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM

Payability Gate

Missing obligation phrase or printed digits

NON_PAYMENT

Sentence Integrity Gate

Label only with no digits, digits outside payable sentence, multiple clauses needed (exception: see below)

MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM

Structural Independence Gate

All five sub-tests must pass (see below)

FIXED_OCCUPATION_PAYMENT

Definition Gate — exceptions
The Definition Gate result of MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM does not apply to:

Ground Rent Floor (Exception 2)

Phased Definition consideration records (Exception 3)

Phased Definition dual appearance definition_only entries in rent_model_terms — these are structural records required by Section 7.3 and are exempt from the Definition Gate

Sentence Integrity Gate — exception
⚠ Schedule Band Mode exception: Schedule Band Mode components require two locations (the introducing clause and the individual band row). This is not a multiple clause violation. Schedule Band Mode components pass the Sentence Integrity Gate. The two-location rule for Schedule Band Mode is governed by Section 6 Evidence Rule 5 and Gate 2.

Structural Independence Gate — all five must be true
#

Test

1

Clause expresses the full rent obligation

2

No model-driven label referenced

3

Amount is not part of a formula

4

Amount is not a floor, base, cap, or minimum

5

No MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS (review-language excepted)

⚠ Label Name Check: Label contains minimum / maximum / cap / floor → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM. Unconditional.

Exception: Where the label contains minimum/maximum/cap/floor but the definition contains only sequential fixed amounts across ordinal periods with no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS, apply Exception 3 — Phased Definition (Gate 4) before applying this check. The Label Name Check does not override the Phased Definition Exception.

⚠ Definition Origin Check: Amount first appears in a means/shall mean clause → MODEL_DRIVEN_TERM. Permanent.

⚠ Sub-paragraph Independence: Each sub-paragraph evaluated independently. Model-driven neighbours do not contaminate clean sub-paragraphs.

Section 6 — Record Construction
⚠ Pre-Construction Tripwire: Candidate contains "means"/"shall mean" → stop. Suspended for Phased Definition Exception phases and Exception 7 (Single Fixed Definition with Separate Review) phases.

Evidence Rules (all must pass)
#

Rule

1

internal_evidence must be an array of evidence objects.

Format:



"internal_evidence": [
  {
    "page": 12,
    "clause": "4.2",
    "text": "The Tenant shall pay the Yearly Rent of £50,000"
  }
]
Rules:

Each evidence object represents exactly one source location.

Each evidence object must contain:

page — the page number where the evidence appears.

clause — the printed clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, or other reference. Use "" if no reference is printed.

text — the shortest verbatim extract sufficient to support the extraction.

The first evidence object must contain the payable sentence supporting the record and must include the obligation wording and the printed digits matching payment_amount.

Evidence Purity Rules
Apply the gate to the text field only.

Do not evaluate page or clause.

Each evidence object is evaluated independently.

A single failing evidence object causes the entire record to fail.

Evidence objects must contain verbatim source wording only.

Evidence objects must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, interpretations, page references, clause references, or extracted conclusions.

payment_dates
Where the payable clause prints specific payment dates (e.g. "the usual quarter days", "25 March, 24 June, 29 September and 25 December") → capture verbatim.

Start Date Anchor (rent / ground rent / base rent)
CRITICAL: The extractor does not perform date arithmetic. If the start date is not printed as a full calendar date, leave payment_start_date blank and capture the mechanism wording in notes.

State

Rule

State 1 — Printed date

A full calendar date is explicitly printed in the payable sentence or the relevant definition (e.g. "31 March 2017", "from 1 January 2024"). Copy exactly in ISO format.

State 2 — Named date or defined term

The start date is expressed as a named date, defined term, or reference to another date. If the named date resolves to a printed calendar date elsewhere in the document → copy the printed date. Do NOT record a start_date_mechanism: entry. If the named date resolves to relative or anniversary wording, or cannot be resolved to a printed date → leave payment_start_date blank. Record the verbatim wording in notes as start_date_mechanism:.

State 3 — "Date hereof" / "date of this Lease"

Scan recital/testimonium for execution date. Full day/month/year found as printed characters → copy in ISO format. Do NOT record a start_date_mechanism: entry. Blank or partial → leave payment_start_date blank. Record start_date_mechanism: the date of this Lease in notes. If execution date partially legible, add note: execution_date_partial.

State 4 — Trigger-contingent

Start date depends on an event or condition that has not occurred or cannot be dated from the document → leave payment_start_date blank. Record the trigger in notes (e.g. start_date_mechanism: the Development Completion Date).

⚠ Blank start date with no start_date_mechanism: in notes → delete.

One-level resolution rule (CRITICAL)
When resolving a named date or defined term in State 2, resolve ONE level only.

If the definition contains a printed calendar date → copy it into payment_start_date.

If the definition contains relative or anniversary wording → STOP. Do not look up the anchor date. Do not calculate. Leave payment_start_date blank and record the mechanism.

If the definition references another defined term → follow that term. Continue until you reach either a printed calendar date or relative/anniversary wording. You never calculate past relative wording.

Example — printed date resolves (date populated, no mechanism):



Payable sentence says "from the Rent Commencement Date"
→ Definition says "Rent Commencement Date means 31 March 2017"
→ Printed calendar date found
→ payment_start_date: "2017-03-31"
→ notes: (no start_date_mechanism entry)
Example — relative wording, stop (date blank, mechanism recorded):



Payable sentence says "from the Rent Commencement Date"
→ Definition says "Rent Commencement Date means the first
   anniversary of the Term Commencement Date"
→ Anniversary wording → STOP
→ payment_start_date: ""
→ notes: "start_date_mechanism: the first anniversary of
   the Term Commencement Date"
End Date Anchor
CRITICAL: The extractor does not perform date arithmetic. "The day before X" is a calculation — do not perform it.

State

Rule

State 1 — Printed date

A full calendar date is explicitly printed. Copy exactly in ISO format. Do NOT record an end_date_mechanism: entry.

State 2 — Named date or relative expression

If the expression points directly to a printed calendar date with no arithmetic required → copy. Do NOT record an end_date_mechanism: entry. If the expression requires any calculation — including "the day before X", "X minus 1 day", subtraction, addition, or any other arithmetic → leave payment_end_date blank. Record the verbatim wording in notes as end_date_mechanism:.

State 3 — Final phase

If this record is the final phase and the term end date is printed as a full calendar date elsewhere in the document → copy the printed term end date. Do NOT record an end_date_mechanism: entry. If the term end date is defined by relative wording → leave payment_end_date blank. Record end_date_mechanism: the expiry of the Term in notes.

State 4 — Trigger-contingent

End date depends on an event → leave payment_end_date blank. Record the trigger in notes.

The "day before" prohibition (CRITICAL)
The lease may say "until the date before the Rent Commencement Date." The Rent Commencement Date may be printed as 31 March 2017. The AI must NOT calculate 30 March 2017.

Instead:

payment_end_date: ""

notes include: end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date

The tool layer performs the subtraction.

One-level resolution rule
The same rule as Start Date Anchor applies. Follow defined terms until you reach either a printed calendar date (with no arithmetic needed) or relative/anniversary wording. Whichever you hit first determines the outcome. Never calculate.

Mutual exclusivity rule (CRITICAL — zero tolerance)
A populated date field and a mechanism entry in notes for the same date cannot coexist. They are mutually exclusive.

If the date is printed → populate the date field. Do NOT record a mechanism.

If the date requires calculation → leave the date field blank. Record the mechanism in notes.

Never do both.

WRONG (mutual exclusivity violation):



"Until the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
→ payment_end_date: "2017-03-30"  ← CALCULATED — DELETE THIS
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the
   Rent Commencement Date"  ← KEEP THIS
Corrected:
→ payment_end_date: ""
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the
   Rent Commencement Date"
CORRECT (printed date, no mechanism):



"From 31 March 2017"
→ payment_start_date: "2017-03-31"
→ notes: (no start_date_mechanism entry)
CORRECT (mechanism, no date):



"Until the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
→ payment_end_date: ""
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the
   Rent Commencement Date"
Notes Rules
Must not: name a defined label as fixed rent | insert digits not in the payable sentence | override classification | quote lease wording | cite clauses or pages. Plain business language only — no internal prompt terms.

start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: entries are permitted in notes only when the corresponding date field is blank. These entries must contain verbatim wording from the document describing how the date is defined. They must not contain calculated dates. If the corresponding date field is populated, the mechanism entry must NOT be present.

Ordering in notes: condition: entries first (if any), then start_date_mechanism: / end_date_mechanism: entries, then any other notes content.

Conditional Fixed Phase Rule
For conditional rent provisions — where a fixed amount only applies if a specified event occurs — see Section 0B. The condition field must be populated per the rules there.

condition field population

Section 7 — rent_model_terms
Captures model-driven occupation rent mechanisms only.

⚠ Never capture: service charge, insurance rent, non-occupation payments, rent review mechanisms, interest, or penalties.

7.1 — Entry Gate
All conditions must pass. Failure at any condition → DELETE. No exceptions.

#

Condition

1

No enforcement wording in mechanism text, heading, or schedule title

2

No NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET match

3

No REVIEW_KILL_SET match

4

Mechanism governs occupation rent only — not cost recovery, services, or enforcement

5

Contains at least one MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER (excluding review-language alone) in the definition's own text — not merely in a label it references. The trigger must appear in the words of this definition, not inherited from an upstream or downstream definition

6

Does not operate as a rent review mechanism

⚠ Condition 5 — Phased Definition Exception A definition_only entry created under the Phased Definition dual appearance rule (see Section 7.3 Exception) is exempt from condition 5. A fixed phased rent schedule containing no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS may produce one definition_only entry in rent_model_terms without satisfying condition 5. This entry documents the phase structure for the tool layer. It is not a model-driven mechanism — it is a structural record.

This exception applies only to definition_only entries produced under the dual appearance rule. No other term_type is exempt from condition 5.

⚠ Conditions 1, 2 and 4 — Enforcement and cost recovery: auto-fail examples The following clause types fail conditions 1, 2, and 4 unconditionally. They must never produce a rent_model_terms entry regardless of how they are labelled, where they appear in the lease, or whether they contain MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS:

Interest on late or overdue payments — any clause referencing "judgment", "arbitration award", "base rate", "compounded", "before and after judgment", "overdue", "unpaid", "default rate", or "penalty"

Service charge definitions or instalment mechanisms — any clause referencing "service costs", "service charge", "on account of", "interim instalment", "interim service charge", or "final service charge"

Insurance rent or insurance premium calculations — any clause defining what the tenant pays toward insurance

Administration, management, or estate charges — any clause defining management fees, administration charges, or estate charges

Cost recovery, reimbursement, or indemnity mechanisms — any clause defining what the tenant reimburses or contributes toward landlord expenditure

If the clause heading, schedule title, or definition wording contains any of these descriptors → delete without further evaluation. The presence of a percentage, formula, or other MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER in such a clause does not rescue it. The payment nature governs, not the mechanism type.

⚠ REVIEW_KILL_SET (case-insensitive, full-phrase match only)

"review date" | "rent review" | "review period" | "date of review" | "dates of review" | "open market review" | "surveyor determination" | "arbitration" | "RICS appointment" | "upward only review" | "reviewed rent"

Applied only here and in Section 7.5 Output Guard — never to consideration records.

⚠ Cross-Reference Carve-Out A clause stating rent is "reviewed in accordance with [schedule of another instrument]" describes how this lease's rent operates — not an operative review clause. REVIEW_KILL_SET does not apply. Capture as definition_only.

⚠ Definition-Only Screen A definition that merely names, labels, or describes a period, date, condition, premises, or percentage rate — without containing its own operative formula or calculation — fails condition 5 and must not produce an entry.

Examples of definitions that fail:

"Rent Period means..."

"Relevant Condition means..."

"the Agreed Percentage means 5%"

"Quarterly Payment means..."

"Minimum Rent Review Date means..."

These are supporting definitions consumed by other mechanisms, not rent mechanisms in their own right.

Exception: The Definition-Only Screen does not apply to definition_only entries created under the Phased Definition dual appearance rule. Those entries are structural records required by Section 7.3 and are exempt from this screen.

7.2 — Scope Restriction
Extract only from paragraphs defining how occupation rent is calculated. Do not extract from: rent review schedules, review date clauses, open market provisions, surveyor determination, service charge, insurance, repair/maintenance, cost recovery, general payment clauses, or any schedule/section governing services, costs, insurance, or charges regardless of content.

⚠ Schedule Source Test Identify the schedule or section before extracting. If it governs anything other than occupation rent calculation → do not extract.

The following schedule and section types always fail the scope test:

Schedule/section type Why it fails Interest or default payment section Enforcement mechanism — fails conditions 1 and 2 Service charge schedule or landlord expenditure recovery section Cost recovery — fails conditions 2 and 4 Insurance schedule or clause defining insurance rent Non-occupation payment — fails conditions 2 and 4 Any schedule titled "Services", "Service Costs", "Landlord's Obligations", or "Management" Governs services or cost recovery — fails condition 4 Repair and maintenance provisions Cost recovery — fails condition 4 General payment clauses governing what happens on late payment Enforcement — fails condition 1

Identifying the source schedule is mandatory before extracting any entry. State the schedule name or section heading in internal_evidence. If the source cannot be identified → do not extract.

7.3 — Structural Separation
A paragraph captured as rent_model_terms as a model-driven mechanism must not also contribute its amounts to consideration as fixed occupation payments. Model mechanisms and fixed payments remain structurally separate.

⚠ Exception — Phased Definition dual appearance Where a fixed phased rent schedule produces both consideration records (one per phase) and a mandatory definition_only entry in rent_model_terms, this is not a structural separation violation.

The consideration records capture the payable obligation for each phase

The definition_only entry captures the schedule structure for the tool layer

Both are required. Both are correct. The same amounts appearing in both arrays in this specific pattern is explicitly permitted.

The dual appearance rule in full:

Phases producing consideration records must also have a definition_only entry in rent_model_terms describing every phase in mechanism_summary — amounts, currency, basis, and ordinal period for each phase. Empty rent_model_terms when phased consideration records exist = capture failure.

A definition_only entry describing multiple phased amounts must also produce consideration records — one per phase evaluated through the gate engine. A definition_only entry without corresponding consideration records = capture failure.

The definition_only entry and the consideration records are not alternatives. Both must exist.

This exception applies only where the definition_only entry carries term_type = "definition_only". It does not permit any other term_type to appear in both arrays simultaneously.

7.4 — Component List Rule
Before producing rent_model_terms, build component_list — one entry per distinct component (name + term_type).

Scope: The component_list must include every defined label in the rent schedule that contains a MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER, not only labels referenced in the rent reservation clause. Scan every definition paragraph in the rent schedule and any schedule cross-referenced by it. A label that feeds into another component as an input (e.g. Notional Income feeding into Divisible Rents) is a distinct component and must have its own entry.

Count of rent_model_terms entries must equal component_list count plus any definition_only entries required under the Phased Definition dual appearance rule in Section 7.3. Mismatch → capture failure.

⚠ Dependency Chain Walk Starting from the rent reservation clause, trace every defined label it references. For each label, read the definition. If that definition references further defined labels, read those definitions too. Continue until no new labels are found. Every label in the chain whose definition contains any MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER gets its own component_list entry.

⚠ Parallel Rent Structure Aggregate of components → evaluate each independently. Formula component does not make a parallel fixed component model-driven.

⚠ Umbrella Definition Rule The rent reservation clause that states Rent = aggregate of named components is the umbrella. Exclude it from the component_list. The components it references must each have their own entry. Only the top-level rent reservation definition qualifies as an umbrella. Intermediate aggregating definitions are components, not umbrellas, and must have their own entry.

⚠ Evidence Rule Each entry covers one component only. Maximum two source locations. Exceeds two → split.

⚠ Single-Component Enforcement If mechanism_summary describes more than one named rent component, the entry violates the one-component-per-entry rule. Split immediately.

Permitted term_type values

formula | percentage | arithmetic_progression | definition_only | turnover_based | indexation | umbrella_total

7.6 — Component Relationship Labelling
When two or more rent_model_terms entries describe rent components that interact with each other, mechanism_summary must begin with one of the following relationship labels:

Label

Meaning

Lease wording to look for

HIGHER_OF:

Only the higher of the components applies. The downstream tool evaluates both and returns the greater.

"whichever is the greater of", "the higher of (a) and (b)", "the rent shall be the greater of"

TOP_UP:

Both components are payable, but the variable component only kicks in above the fixed component. The fixed amount is always paid; the variable part tops it up if it exceeds the fixed. Total is always at least the fixed component. Both the fixed floor and variable top-up carry the TOP_UP label.

"the aggregate of (a) the Minimum Rent and (b) the amount by which X% of [income] exceeds the Minimum Rent", "Supplemental Rent means the excess of"

FIXED_PLUS_VARIABLE:

Both components are payable in full, stacked on top of each other. The fixed component is NOT deducted from the variable calculation.

"the Minimum Rent plus X% of [income]" — no "exceeds" or "excess of" language

VALUATION_BASED:

Rent is a percentage of a hypothetical valuation, not of actual income. The tenant's actual receipts are irrelevant.

"X% of the Rental Value" where Rental Value is defined as open market valuation

STANDALONE:

The component operates independently — not linked to another component by a higher-of, aggregate, or additive relationship.

Standard formula, indexation, or turnover mechanism with no cross-reference to another rent component

Rules:

Every rent_model_terms entry must begin mechanism_summary with exactly one label followed by a colon and space, then the plain-language description

If only one rent_model_terms entry exists and it has no relationship to another component, use STANDALONE:

If the relationship cannot be determined from the lease wording, use STANDALONE: and note the ambiguity in notes

Two entries describing opposite sides of the same relationship must use the same label

Example: "HIGHER_OF: The rent is the greater of the Ground Rent (£100,000 per annum) and 5% of the Rents Receivable from all subtenants."

7.7 — Specific External Input Descriptions
When requires_external_inputs = true, the external_inputs array must contain entries specific enough for the downstream tool to identify what is missing.

Dependency

external_inputs entry

Actual subletting income from subtenants

"Subletting income: actual rents received from subtenants for [Rent Period label if printed]"

Deemed/notional income for self-occupied or vacant parts

"Deemed income: open market rental value of self-occupied or vacant parts per [provision label if printed]"

Expense deductions (where Divisible Rents = income minus expenses)

"Expense deductions: actual expenses incurred by tenant for [Rent Period label if printed]"

Prior period income for backward-looking calculation

"Prior period income: actual Divisible Rents for preceding [X]-year period"

Rent amount cross-referenced to a separate document

"External agreement: rent amount defined in [document name as printed in the lease]"

Open market rental valuation

"Valuation: open market rental value of the premises as at [review date or valuation date if printed]"

Do not invent dependency descriptions. If the lease prints a specific label for the income period, expense category, or external document, use that label. If not, use the generic descriptions above.

⚠ Generic entries like "external data required" are insufficient.

7.8 — Deemed Income Provisions
Where the lease contains provisions imputing income for self-occupied or vacant parts — look for "Notional Income", "Deemed Underlease", "Relevant Condition", "Full Rack Rental Value" triggered by self-occupation or vacancy:

Extract a rent_model_terms entry with term_type = formula or percentage (whichever matches the mechanism)

mechanism_summary must include "Deemed income provisions apply for self-occupation or vacancy" in the description (after the relationship label from 7.6)

requires_external_inputs = true

external_inputs must include the deemed income entry from 7.7

If the lease specifies a grace period before deemed income triggers, record the grace period in notes

7.9 — Estimate and Certificate Payment Patterns
Where the lease provides for rent to be paid on provisional estimates with subsequent correction — look for "Rent Estimate", "Corrected Estimate", "Interim Statement", "Annual Statement", "Final Certificate", "Rent Certificate":

Extract a rent_model_terms entry with term_type = formula

mechanism_summary: after the relationship label, describe the payment pattern (e.g. "Rent paid on forward estimates, adjusted via Corrected Estimates, finalised by Final Certificate after year-end")

notes must state: "Current rent may be provisional — subject to reconciliation via [certificate mechanism as printed]."

This does not affect any consideration record — the fixed floor (if any) is still extracted normally through the gate engine

7.10 — Phase-Dependent Rent Formulas
Where the rent schedule has multiple "Parts" governing different periods with different formulas (e.g. pre-development, operational, redevelopment, post-redevelopment):

Extract one rent_model_terms entry per phase

Each entry's mechanism_summary must state which phase it governs (after the relationship label from 7.6): e.g. "STANDALONE: Pre-development phase — peppercorn rent until the Development Completion Date."

If the phase boundary is event-driven (Certificate Date, Development Letting Date, Practical Completion Date), record the event in notes

If the phase boundary date is unknown: requires_external_inputs = true, and include in external_inputs: "Phase boundary date: [event name as printed in the lease]"

8. Phase Computation
CRITICAL: The extractor does not perform date arithmetic. All date calculation is the tool layer's responsibility. The extractor copies printed dates and captures mechanism wording. Nothing more.

Date determinability never affects record validity.

Mode A — Explicit Printed Dates
Date printed in full day/month/year → copy exactly (ISO conversion only). No adjustment, inference, or calculation. Applies independently to start and end dates. Explicit dates override all other modes.

Mode B — Ordinal Anniversary Bands (Mechanism Capture)
Where a rent schedule contains sequential ordinal bands (e.g. "years 1–3", "years 4–6", "years 7–10", or "first five years", "second five years"), the AI captures the band structure. The tool layer calculates the actual dates.

What the AI captures
For each banded phase, populate the record as follows:

Field

Rule

payment_start_date

Copy only if a full calendar date is printed for this specific phase. If the phase is described by ordinal wording only (e.g. "from the commencement of the fourth year") → leave blank.

payment_end_date

Copy only if a full calendar date is printed for this specific phase. If the phase boundary is described by ordinal wording only (e.g. "until the expiry of the sixth year") → leave blank.

start_date_mechanism: in notes

The band start description as printed (e.g. start_date_mechanism: the commencement of the fourth year of the Term). Required when payment_start_date is blank.

end_date_mechanism: in notes

The band end description as printed (e.g. end_date_mechanism: the expiry of the sixth year of the Term). Required when payment_end_date is blank.

⚠ Both entries are mandatory. A record with start_date_mechanism: but no end_date_mechanism: is incomplete. For every ordinal band record, both must be present in notes. Missing end_date_mechanism: on any non-final phase → reprocess that record before returning output.

What the AI does NOT do
Does not take a commencement date and add years

Does not calculate "Phase n start = C + ((n−1) × L)"

Does not subtract days to produce end dates

Does not convert ordinal bands ("years 4–6") into calendar dates

Does not calculate "C + T − 1 day" for the final phase end date

Does not import the lease commencement date from the demise clause or recitals to populate payment_start_date for the first ordinal band. The first band starts at lease commencement by implication — but that date is not printed in the rent schedule. Leave payment_start_date blank and record start_date_mechanism: commencement of the [first/second/etc.] twenty-five years of the lease period in notes. The tool layer supplies the commencement date.

What the tool layer receives
The tool layer gets:

The commencement date from Key Dates

The term length from Key Dates

The band structure from start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: entries in notes on each record

The number of phases and their sequence from the record order

The tool layer uses these inputs to calculate all phase start and end dates.

Completeness rule
Every phase in the rent schedule must produce a record. If the schedule has N bands, the output must have N records (each evaluated through the gate engine). Missing phases → reprocess.

Worked example
Lease: 10-year term commencing 25 June 2016. Rent schedule has three bands: "years 1–3 at £10,000 p.a.", "years 4–6 at £12,000 p.a.", "years 7–10 at £15,000 p.a."

Record 1:

payment_amount: 10000

payment_start_date: "" (not printed as a calendar date)

payment_end_date: "" (not printed as a calendar date)

notes: start_date_mechanism: commencement of the Term. end_date_mechanism: the expiry of the third year of the Term.

Record 2:

payment_amount: 12000

payment_start_date: ""

payment_end_date: ""

notes: start_date_mechanism: the commencement of the fourth year of the Term. end_date_mechanism: the expiry of the sixth year of the Term.

Record 3:

payment_amount: 15000

payment_start_date: ""

payment_end_date: ""

notes: start_date_mechanism: the commencement of the seventh year of the Term. end_date_mechanism: the expiry of the Term.

The tool layer resolves: Record 1 = 25 June 2016 to 24 June 2019. Record 2 = 25 June 2019 to 24 June 2022. Record 3 = 25 June 2022 to 24 June 2026. The extractor never produces these dates.

Mode C — No Determinable Dates
If neither Mode A nor Mode B applies → leave date fields blank. Record whatever timing wording exists in start_date_mechanism: / end_date_mechanism: in notes. Date determinability never affects record validity.
