Peppercorn: payment_amount must be 0, currency must be "". Non-zero → delete and recreate zeroed. Also: if any record has payment_amount = 0 and consideration_type is NOT "peppercorn rent" → check the definition. If it contains "peppercorn" → reclassify.

2

Type must be a permitted occupation type → else delete.

3

Amount must be numeric and > 0. Does not apply to peppercorn rent.

4

No null values in any field.

⚠ Fail-Safe Output Purge: After all validation, if any output field matches NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET after normalisation → purge.

10. Worked Examples
Example 1 — Multi-phase reddendum with "greater of" review
Lease structure: 10-year term, three-phase reddendum. Phase 1: peppercorn from lease date to First Rent Commencement Date. Phase 2: Reduced Basic Rent (£248,422 p.a.) from First Rent Commencement Date to Second Rent Commencement Date. Phase 3: Basic Rent (£496,844 p.a.) from Second Rent Commencement Date, reviewed per Schedule 5. Schedule 5 reviews to "the greater of" the passing rent and open market rent. Landlord pays tenant £5,367.23 contribution on lease date described as a reverse premium.

Key reasoning:

Decision

Why

Phase 3 (Basic Rent £496,844) excluded from consideration

Schedule 5 contains "greater of" — a MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER (Check C). Appears only in the definition_only rent_model_terms entry.

Phase 1 (peppercorn) captured

Completeness Rule: 3 reddendum sub-paragraphs requires 3 consideration records evaluated.

Phase 2 (Reduced Basic Rent £248,422) passes as consideration

Its reddendum sub-paragraph contains no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS. Type is "rent" not "base rent" — the lease labels it "Reduced Basic Rent" which is a distinct label.

Reverse premium survives the kill set

Via the reverse premium carve-out: direction is landlord to tenant, fixed lump sum, connected to lease grant.

internal_evidence must be concise

The relevant sub-paragraph and Particulars row only. Never paste the entire demise clause.

Example 2 — Premium with formula-only rent
Lease structure: Premium acknowledged in demise clause. Rent is the "greater of" a fixed floor and a percentage-based figure, then shifts to an aggregate formula. No fixed rent phase survives.

Key reasoning:

Decision

Why

The £9,600 is inside a "greater of" comparison

Check C kills it from consideration. Appears only as fixed_amount_printed in rent_model_terms.

Check A

If notes say "excluded" or "model-driven" but the record exists in consideration → delete. No exceptions.

Rent review mechanism caught by REVIEW_KILL_SET

Entry Gate 7.1(3) and 7.1(6) both apply. No rent_model_terms entry.

Premium passes via Recital Receipt Mode

internal_evidence is the recital sentence only — not the full demise clause.

Example 3 — Split sentence: fixed phase followed by formula phase
Lease structure: Rent clause in a single sentence. Fixed rent of £400 p.a. from lease date until Rent Commencement Date, then "and thereafter" the greater of Ground Rent and Landlord's Percentage of Rents Receivable. LR7 Premium is one peppercorn.

Key reasoning:

Decision

Why

Split Sentence Kill applies

"and thereafter" joins a fixed phase (£400) and a formula phase (greater of).

Fixed phase (£400) extractable as consideration

Printed digits, temporally distinct, no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS of its own.

Formula phase after "and thereafter" → rent_model_terms only

"greater of" is a MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER (Check C).

Self-check item 8

Count every distinct rent amount including amounts embedded within the same sentence. £400 and the formula phase = two phases, two records evaluated.

End date for £400 phase

Trigger-contingent on Rent Commencement Date — leave blank, record trigger in notes.

One addition only — a worked example in Section 10.

Where to paste: Section 10 — Worked Examples Paste after Example 3 as Example 4

Example 4 — Premium embedded in opening operative clause with surrender language
Lease structure: The opening operative clause states that the landlord lets the property in consideration of two things: (1) the surrender by the tenant of a prior lease, and (2) receipt of a lump sum of £125,000 paid by the tenant. No separate premium clause exists. The lump sum appears only in this single sentence. The remainder of the lease contains a stepped rent schedule and service charge provisions.

Decision

Why

Full document scan must be completed before classifying any component

The scan phase is mandatory and must cover every page. The opening operative clause is on page 2. If the scan stops at the rent schedule, the premium is missed. Scan triggers "having received" and "which the Tenants paid" both match this clause — it must be collected in the scan phase

Premium extracted via Recital Receipt Mode

The opening operative clause explicitly satisfies condition 4 of Recital Receipt Mode — permitted locations are recitals, opening operative clause, and testimonium. All four conditions are met: receipt phrase present, fixed amount in digits, currency indicator present, sentence in opening operative clause

Surrender language does not kill the premium

The NON_OCCUPATION_KILL_SET does not apply. The surrender describes the context of payment — not the nature of the payment itself. The £125,000 is a fixed lump sum paid by the tenant to the landlord connected to the grant of this lease. It passes the reverse premium carve-out test in reverse — direction is tenant to landlord, so it is a premium not a reverse premium

consideration_type = "premium"

Type Selection Rule order 1 — premium identified first. Direction is tenant to landlord

payment_frequency, payment_timing, amount_basis = "not_stated"

Recital Receipt Mode premium field rules apply unconditionally

payment_start_date = ""

Execution date in this lease is partially legible — leave blank per State 3 rules. Record execution_date_partial in notes

internal_evidence = opening operative clause sentence only

Must end at the first sentence boundary after the receipt acknowledgement. Do not include the habendum, demise wording, or rent reservations

Self-Check item 32

After completing all records, verify at least one premium or reverse premium record exists if any scan trigger from Section 3 matched during the scan phase. If a scan trigger matched but no premium record exists → re-scan pages 1–5 for receipt language before returning output. Do not return output without resolving this

 

Section 11 — Self-Check (run before returning JSON) — HARD
Every check below is mandatory. Execute in order. Any failure → correct before returning. No exceptions.

Dates
#

Check

1

Where both payment_start_date and payment_end_date are populated on the same record: is payment_end_date before payment_start_date? If yes → dates are wrong. Correct or blank both and record the issue in notes.

2

Where consecutive phases both have populated dates: do they visually align (Phase n end date should be one day before Phase n+1 start date)? The AI does not calculate this. If both dates are printed and visually don't align → flag in notes as phase_date_alignment_query. The tool layer validates alignment.

3

Does the lease date in the document match the year used in all populated start and end dates? A lease dated 1977 cannot produce dates in 1997. Verify every populated date against the source.

4

Is any date trigger-contingent? If yes → field must be blank with the trigger recorded in notes as start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism:. Never guess.

Anti-calculation checks (CRITICAL)
#

Check

5

No date in payment_start_date or payment_end_date was derived from relative, anniversary, ordinal band, or "day before" wording. If any was → blank the field and populate the corresponding start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes.

6

Every blank payment_start_date has a start_date_mechanism: entry in notes (unless the record is a peppercorn with a printed start date or a premium). Blank date with no mechanism → delete or investigate.

7

Every blank payment_end_date on a non-final phase has an end_date_mechanism: entry in notes. Blank date with no mechanism on a mid-phase record → delete or investigate.

8

No start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes contains a calculated calendar date. These entries must contain verbatim document wording only — never a resolved date.

9

Where a named date or defined term was resolved to populate a date field: verify the resolution reached a printed calendar date, not relative or anniversary wording. If the definition contains anniversary or relative wording and the date field is populated → blank the field and record the mechanism.

10

Mutual exclusivity check (zero tolerance): If payment_start_date is populated AND notes contain start_date_mechanism: → VIOLATION. The date was calculated. Set payment_start_date to "". Keep the mechanism. If payment_end_date is populated AND notes contain end_date_mechanism: → VIOLATION. The date was calculated. Set payment_end_date to "". Keep the mechanism. A populated date field and a mechanism entry for the same field cannot coexist. If both are present, the date was derived — delete the date, keep the mechanism.

Payment terms
#

Check

Peppercorn

For every record with payment_amount = 0, verify consideration_type is "peppercorn rent". Follow the definition chain: payable sentence → label → definition. If the definition says "peppercorn", the Peppercorn Rule applies.

Base Rent

Is any record classified as "base rent"? Verify the lease label is literally "Base Rent". Labels such as "Principal Rent", "Yearly Rent", "Annual Rent" are NOT base rent — they are "rent" (or "peppercorn rent" if the definition contains "peppercorn").

11a

Does amount_basis match how the payable sentence expresses the rent amount (e.g. "per annum" = yearly, "per month" = monthly)?

11b

Does payment_frequency match how often the tenant actually makes payments as stated in the payable sentence (e.g. "quarterly" / "on the usual quarter days" = quarterly, "monthly" = monthly)? payment_frequency is not the same as amount_basis — a rent expressed yearly but paid quarterly has amount_basis: "yearly" and payment_frequency: "quarterly".

12

If the lease prints specific payment dates, is payment_dates populated?

13

For premiums and reverse premiums: are payment_frequency, payment_timing, and amount_basis all "not_stated"? Also verify: for every premium and reverse premium record where payment_start_date is blank — confirm this is because the execution date was partially legible or not determinable (State 3 rules in Section 6 apply). A blank payment_start_date on a premium is correct only when the execution date cannot be determined from the document. If the execution date is printed as a full calendar date elsewhere in the document, payment_start_date should be populated.

Evidence and notes
#

Check

14

Does at least one internal_evidence.text value contain the exact digit string matching payment_amount? Not found → the record is wrong. Delete or correct.

15

Do notes contain page numbers, clause numbers, section names, schedule references? Found → remove. (Exception: start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: entries may contain clause-level defined term names like "Rent Commencement Date" but must not contain page or clause number references.)

16

Does any consideration record have notes containing "excluded" or "model-driven" while the record exists? If yes → delete the record entirely (Check A).

17

Do notes follow the correct ordering? condition: entries first, then start_date_mechanism: / end_date_mechanism: entries, then any other notes content. Out of order → correct.

18

If a start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry exists in notes AND the corresponding date field is populated → the mechanism entry should not be present (mutual exclusivity). Delete the mechanism entry OR blank the date field — but they cannot both remain. Per the mutual exclusivity rule in Section 6, delete the date and keep the mechanism.

Completeness
#

Check

19

HARD STOP — Count every distinct rent amount in the rent schedule or reddendum, including peppercorn periods, rent-free periods, reduced rent periods, and fixed amounts embedded within the same sentence as a formula phase. The count of consideration records evaluated (including records that failed the gates) must equal the count of distinct rent amounts. If the counts do not match, stop immediately. Do not return any output. Reprocess the rent schedule applying Schedule Band Mode (Gate 2) and Exception 3 (Gate 4) before returning.

⚠ rent_model_terms does not satisfy this count. A definition_only entry in rent_model_terms describing multiple phased amounts is not a substitute for the corresponding number of consideration records. The count applies to consideration records only. If the rent schedule contains N distinct amounts, N consideration records must exist — or N records must have been evaluated and failed the gates with the failure reason recorded. A single definition_only entry covering all phases with zero consideration records = count failure. Hard stop applies.

#

Check

20

Does the YIELDING AND PAYING / reddendum clause reserve any fixed sums not captured in consideration?

20A

Exception 7 rescue check: Does the reddendum or payable clause reference a defined rent label whose definition prints exactly one fixed amount with no MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGERS in the definition text, but no consideration record exists for that amount? Check: (1) the definition is a clean fixed sum — no percentage, greater of, index, formula, calculated, or any other MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER in the definition's own wording, (2) a separate payable clause with an obligation phrase references the label, (3) any model language exists only in a separate review schedule or review clause. If all three are true and no consideration record captures the fixed amount → apply Exception 7 (Gate 4) and create the record. Empty consideration for this pattern = capture failure.

Model-driven filtering
#

Check

21

Does any consideration record reference a defined label whose definition contains a MODEL_LANGUAGE_TRIGGER? If yes → delete from consideration, ensure it appears in rent_model_terms. Exception 7 override: Do NOT delete if the record was created via Exception 7 — where the definition itself is clean of triggers and the only model language is in a separate review schedule. In this case the record is valid. The trigger fires in Check 21 only when the definition's own text contains the trigger, not when a separate schedule does.

22

Does any consideration record contain a payment_amount that does not appear as printed digits in the lease for that phase? A figure copied from a different phase or invented from a formula is fabricated. Delete. After any deletion under this check, re-run item 19 to verify the count remains consistent. If deletion causes the count to fall below the number of distinct rent amounts and no gate failure reason exists for the deleted record, investigate before returning output.

rent_model_terms checks
#

Check

23

Does any rent_model_terms entry describe a rent review mechanism or a non-head-lease rent obligation? If yes → delete.

24

Does any rent_model_terms entry contain a fixed_amount_printed value? Verify it matches the digits printed in the lease exactly.

25

For every entry: does term_type match the mechanism? Mismatch → correct.

Chain tracking
#

Check

26

consideration_change_type must be one of the five permitted values on every record. Must not be "" on any actual record.

27

For "variation" records: all fields populated with new terms. targets_consideration_description must identify the original. Record must be complete and self-contained.

28

For "removal" records: consideration_type and payment_amount must identify the component being removed. targets_consideration_description must identify the original.

29

Each record's internal_source must identify which document it comes from.

30

targets_consideration_description must be populated on "variation" and "removal" records. Must be "" on all others.

31

targets_consideration_description must be specific enough to distinguish between multiple rent components.

32

At least one consideration record with consideration_change_type = "original" must exist if the original lease contains rent provisions.

Conditional consideration
#

Check

33

Every consideration record has a condition field that is a string. Records with no conditionality have condition: "". Records contingent on an event have condition populated with a plain-language description of the trigger. No record has condition as null, undefined, or missing.

34

If a conditional record exists for the same period as an unconditional record, verify they represent different obligations (e.g. standard rent vs reduced rent if break not exercised). A conditional record must not be a duplicate of an unconditional record with an unnecessary condition added.

rent_model_terms and contingent_consideration — Complex Mechanisms
#

Check

35

Does every rent_model_terms entry begin mechanism_summary with a relationship label (HIGHER_OF:, TOP_UP:, FIXED_PLUS_VARIABLE:, VALUATION_BASED:, or STANDALONE:)? Missing label → add per Section 7.6.

36

If two or more rent_model_terms entries reference each other (for example both sides of a greater-of calculation), do they carry the same relationship label? Contradictory labels → correct.

37

For every rent_model_terms entry with requires_external_inputs = true: does external_inputs contain at least one entry using the standard descriptions from Section 7.7? Generic entries → replace with the specific dependency.

38

If the lease contains provisions for deemed or notional income arising from self-occupation or vacancy (Notional Income, Deemed Underlease, Relevant Condition, Full Rack Rental Value): does a rent_model_terms entry exist describing the mechanism with requires_external_inputs = true? Missing → extract per Section 7.8.

39

If the lease provides for rent paid on estimates with subsequent correction (Rent Estimate, Corrected Estimate, Interim Statement, Annual Statement, Final Certificate, Rent Certificate): does a rent_model_terms entry exist describing the mechanism? Missing → extract per Section 7.9.

40

If the rent schedule contains multiple Parts, phases, operational periods, development periods, redevelopment periods, or other distinct periods governed by different rent formulae: does a separate rent_model_terms entry exist for each phase? Missing phases → extract per Section 7.10.

41

If the lease contains overage, clawback, uplift, development gain, planning gain, profit share, additional consideration, further consideration, or any similar landlord-benefiting contingent payment mechanism: does a contingent_consideration record exist? Missing → extract per Section 0D.

42

Does every contingent_consideration entry contain a valid mechanism value? Permitted values are: overage, clawback, uplift, profit_share, development_gain, planning_gain, additional_consideration, further_consideration, or other. Invalid or missing mechanism → correct.

43

Does every contingent_consideration entry contain a populated mechanism_summary explaining how the payment is triggered or calculated? Missing summary → capture failure.

44

Does every contingent_consideration entry contain a populated trigger_event describing the event that causes the payment obligation to arise? Missing trigger_event → capture failure.

45

For every contingent_consideration entry with requires_external_inputs = true: does external_inputs identify the specific information required to determine the payment? Generic entries such as "external data required" are insufficient and must be replaced with the specific dependency.

46

Verify that no overage, clawback, uplift, development gain, planning gain, profit share, additional consideration, or further consideration mechanism appears in consideration. If found → remove and reclassify into contingent_consideration.

47

Verify that no overage, clawback, uplift, development gain, planning gain, profit share, additional consideration, or further consideration mechanism appears in rent_model_terms. If found → remove and reclassify into contingent_consideration.

48

Verify that every contingent_consideration entry contains supporting internal_evidence demonstrating: (a) the contingent payment obligation; (b) the mechanism type; and (c) the trigger event where stated. Missing supporting evidence → capture failure.

New Validation Principle
Contingent consideration mechanisms are structurally separate from both fixed consideration and rent calculation mechanisms.



Fixed payment amount → consideration
Rent or licence fee calculation mechanism → rent_model_terms
Overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain,
planning gain, additional consideration and further consideration
→ contingent_consideration
No contingent consideration mechanism may appear in either:

consideration

rent_model_terms

All such mechanisms must be extracted into:

contingent_consideration

Any failure at any check → correct before returning. Do not output uncorrected JSON.

schema.title: Consideration Extraction Schema [v1.4]
schema.description: Schema for extracting lease and licence consideration data from property documents. Version 1.4 separates fixed consideration, rent and licence fee model terms, and contingent consideration mechanisms such as overage, clawback, uplift and profit share.

consideration.description: Array of fixed payment obligation records extracted from the lease or licence. Use this for fixed rent, ground rent, base rent, peppercorn rent, premium, reverse premium, landlord contribution and licence fee records only.

consideration_type.description: The category of fixed payment. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

payment_amount.description: The monetary amount. 0 is permitted for peppercorn rent only. All others must be greater than 0. Landlord-to-tenant payments are recorded as positive amounts.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, for example GBP. Peppercorn rent must be empty string. No currency symbols.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before this fixed payment obligation takes effect. Empty string when unconditional.

amount_basis.description: The basis on which the amount is expressed.

payment_frequency.description: How often payment is made. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where no recurring frequency applies.

payment_timing.description: Whether payment is made in advance or arrears. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where neither applies.

payment_dates.description: Specific dates on which payment falls due. Free text, verbatim from the lease where printed.

payment_start_date.description: Start date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

payment_end_date.description: End date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

rent_commencement_date.description: The date rent or licence fee payments begin. ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

consideration_change_type.description: How this record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original record being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the fixed payment obligation.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted payment obligation. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

rent_model_terms.description: Array of occupation payment adjustment or calculation mechanism records. Use this for rent or licence fee mechanisms only. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

term_type.description: Classification of the rent or licence fee mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the rent or licence fee mechanism works. Must begin with: HIGHER_OF, TOP_UP, FIXED_PLUS_VARIABLE, VALUATION_BASED, or STANDALONE.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Starting amount for arithmetic_progression, or the fixed amount printed in the source clause. Empty string for all other term types or when no fixed digits appear.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism depends on external data, such as index values or turnover figures.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs needed. Empty array if none required.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the rent or licence fee mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

contingent_consideration.description: Array of contingent, deferred, variable or event-triggered consideration payment mechanisms which are not fixed occupation rent and are not rent calculation mechanisms. Use this for overage, clawback, uplift payments, profit share, development gain, planning gain, additional consideration, further consideration and similar landlord-benefiting contingent payments.

consideration_type.description: Use only for contingent consideration obligations that are not fixed rent, premium, licence fee or landlord contribution.

mechanism.description: The specific type of contingent consideration mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the contingent payment is calculated or triggered. Do not calculate the payment.

trigger_event.description: Plain-language description of the event that triggers the payment obligation. Empty string if not clear.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string if no currency is printed.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Any fixed monetary amount printed as part of the contingent mechanism. Empty string if none.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism requires external information to determine the amount payable, such as sale price, profit, valuation, development cost, planning uplift, lettings income or disposal proceeds.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs required to calculate the contingent payment. Empty array if none.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before the contingent payment obligation applies. Empty string when unconditional.

consideration_change_type.description: How this contingent consideration record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original contingent consideration being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the contingent consideration mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted contingent consideration mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

fields.name: consideration_review
fields.description:
[v1.3] Consideration Review — Extraction Prompt 
0. Pre-output failure checks — run before returning JSON
Before returning JSON, execute every check in Section 25 (Self-Check). The following are the highest-risk failures:

Check

Failure

Fix

⚠ A 

Collapsed Multi-Component Structure: rent structure contains N distinct mechanisms at different cadences but output has fewer than N records 

Must contain N separate records. Apply the Concurrent Component Rule (Section 5). 

⚠ B 

Conflated reviewed_rent_components: a record lists components belonging to other mechanisms 

Each record's reviewed_rent_components must name only the component(s) that record's mechanism re-determines. 

⚠ C 

Over-Quoted Evidence: internal_evidence contains more than 5 evidence objects

Must contain only the anchor + minimum supporting segments for timing, mechanism, and constraints. 

⚠ D 

Misclassified review_type: income base classified as market, or market base classified as income 

Income received/receivable → income_linked. Open market rental value → market or formula. Apply the Review Type Self-Test (Section 7). 

⚠ E 

Missing Anchor Resolution: trigger.type = "cycle" or "date" but anchor fields blank without explanation 

Every such record must have anchor_date and anchor_kind populated (or explicitly "" and "unresolvable" — or "" and "relative_mechanism" when the date is defined by anniversary/relative wording). 

⚠ F 

Wrong Pattern Applied to Cycle Anchor: Pattern A/B mismatch 

"X and each anniversary" → Pattern A: anchor_date = cycle_reference_date (the printed reference date, copied — not calculated). "Each anniversary of X" → Pattern B: the first review needs a period added, so anchor_date = "", anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism", review_date_mechanism carries the verbatim wording, and the tool computes the date. The extractor never adds a period to a date. 

⚠ G 

Relative date calculated: a date in trigger.dates or anchor_date was derived from anniversary or relative wording rather than printed in the document. 

anchor_date must be "". anchor_kind must be "relative_mechanism". review_date_mechanism must contain the verbatim timing wording. For a date trigger, trigger.dates must be ["RELATIVE"]; for a cycle trigger, trigger.dates stays empty and the relative signal is carried by anchor_kind. The extractor does not perform date arithmetic. 

0A. Chain document bypass — run before the qualification gates
⚠ This section applies when the document is NOT the original lease but a later document in the chain that modifies an existing rent review mechanism.

Before applying Section 1 and the qualification gates, check: does the operative wording modify, delete, substitute, or introduce a rent review mechanism from an earlier document?

Variation language to look for:

Pattern

"the rent review provisions are hereby varied" / "Schedule [X] is varied as follows"

"the review dates shall be" / "the review cycle is changed to"

"the rent review clause is hereby deleted" / "shall cease to have effect"

"is hereby substituted" / "shall be replaced by" / "in substitution for"

"the index shall be [X] instead of [Y]" / "CPI shall be substituted for RPI"

"the review shall be upward only" / "the upward only provision is removed"

"a new rent review provision is hereby added"

"the cap/floor shall be [X]% instead of [Y]%"

Any wording referencing a review schedule from the original lease and modifying its terms

If variation language is found:

Addition — new review mechanism not in the original lease.

Rule

Detail

review_change_type

"addition"

All fields

Populated from whatever the document prints. Process through standard qualification gates.

Variation — existing review terms changed.

Rule

Detail

review_change_type

"variation"

targets_review_description

Plain-language description of the original review being varied (e.g. "index-linked review tied to RPI").

All fields

Populated with the new terms as they now stand. Record must be complete and self-contained.

Gate engine

For variations restating the full mechanism, process through standard gates. For variations stating only the change, bypass — the variation language is sufficient evidence.

Removal — review mechanism deleted.

Rule

Detail

review_change_type

"removal"

targets_review_description

Plain-language description of the review being removed.

review_type and trigger

Enough information to identify which review is being removed.

Additional rules:

Rule

Detail

No variation language found

Proceed to standard gates. Document may still contain its own review provisions.

Both variation AND new mechanism

Process each separately.

All records from the original lease

review_change_type = "original".

1. Mission
Extract rent re-determination mechanisms only — when and how rent can change during the term. Fixed sums, peppercorn rents, premiums, and service fees belong in the consideration prompt. No duplication between prompts.

For each mechanism, capture both (a) the mechanism as it appears in the lease and (b) the resolved anchor date plus cycle interval so downstream systems can compute the next review date without further document reading — except where the review date is defined by relative or anniversary wording, in which case the extractor captures the verbatim mechanism wording and leaves date calculation to the tool layer.

Return strict JSON only per response_format schema. No intermediate steps. Do not infer, calculate, or reconstruct — except where Section 13A (Anchor Resolution) explicitly permits cross-referencing to a printed calendar date.

2. Pre-Qualification Gate
Before creating any record: does this phase contain a re-determination mechanism?

Rent fixed at a nominal or specified sum → do not extract. Phase end date is not a review trigger. Phase contains a mechanism that sets or recalculates rent → proceed. Where a clause prints both a fixed phase and a subsequent mechanism → apply the gate to each phase independently.

3. Recall-First Rule
Re-determination mechanism present (having survived the gates in Sections 2 and 4) → create a record. Defects affect field population only — never suppress the record. A mechanism the gates have excluded is not "present" for this rule — do not create a record for it.

4. Qualifying Mechanism Test
Both conditions must be true from printed wording: Triggered (fires at a printed date, cycle, or event) and Determinative (sets or recalculates rent by valuation, formula, income method, or fixed rule). If either is absent → does not qualify.

5. Composite Structures
Structure

Rule

Bounded Phase

Fires once at phase start → trigger.type: event. Recurring → trigger.type: cycle. Income-linked referencing each Rent Period → trigger.type: cycle from Rent Period definition.

Rent as the greater of two separately calculated figures → extract a separate record for each mechanism. Set greater_of = true on both. Never collapse into one record.

Valuation Inputs

Each record: exactly one anchor + minimum definitions for timing and mechanism only.

13. Trigger Object (Binding)
Populate from printed wording only. No inference, calculation, or derived dates anywhere in this output. The trigger fields (dates, cycle, event) and the anchor fields (Section 13A) may record a printed calendar date that appears in the document or is reached by cross-reference (a look-up). They must never record a date produced by arithmetic. Adding a period to a reference date, projecting a cycle, or working out an anniversary is the tool's job, not the extractor's.

13.1 — Recurrence Test (wording only — never project dates)
Decide cycle vs date from the wording, never by working out how many reviews fall in the term.

3

Check "Review Period" wording. One period to expiry = one review. Multiple periods = cycle.

4

Default

Ambiguous → classify as date. Upgrade to cycle only with explicit recurrence wording.

Whether a cycle actually produces a second review inside the term is a date projection (arithmetic). That check belongs to the tool, not the extractor. Classify from wording alone.

13A. Anchor Resolution (Binding)
Every trigger of type cycle or date must populate the anchor fields — with resolved values, or explicitly "" where the anchor is relative or unresolvable. Triggers of type event populate where the event's calendar date is printed. Triggers of type unknown leave anchor fields blank.

13A.0 — Anti-calculation gate
Before applying any resolution pathway in 13A.2: ask whether reaching the first review date requires arithmetic.

If the first review date is a printed calendar date (printed directly, or reached by cross-reference — a look-up) → resolve it and record it. This includes Pattern A, where the first review is the printed reference date (a copy, not a calculation).

If the first review date can only be reached by adding a period to another date, or is defined by relative/anniversary wording → do not resolve further. Set:

anchor_date: ""

anchor_kind: relative_mechanism

anchor_reasoning: plain-language description of why the date is left relative (e.g. "Review Date defined as the fifth anniversary of the Term Commencement Date — anniversary wording, resolved by the tool")

review_date_mechanism: the verbatim timing wording

The resolution pathways in 13A.2 apply only when the anchor is itself a printed date. They never produce a calculated anchor.

13A.1 — First Review Date Resolution (Cycle Pattern Classification)
The purpose of this section is to determine whether the first review date can be recorded directly from the document, or whether it must be left for the tool to calculate.

The extractor never performs date arithmetic.

The extractor may:

Copy a printed calendar date.

Resolve a printed calendar date through cross-references.

Record verbatim timing wording.

The extractor must never:

Add years to a date.

Calculate anniversaries.

Generate future review dates.

Pattern A — Reference Date Is The First Review Date
Use Pattern A when the wording states that reviews occur on a named date and then continue at intervals after that date.

Typical wording:

"X and each anniversary thereof"

"X and every fifth anniversary thereafter"

"the Review Date and every fifth anniversary thereafter"

In these cases, the reference date itself is the first review date.

If the reference date resolves to a printed calendar date, populate both:



"cycle_reference_date": "1999-09-28",
"anchor_date": "1999-09-28"
No calculation is performed.

The date is simply copied.

Examples

Wording

cycle_reference_date

anchor_date

"2 November 2001 and each fifth anniversary thereof"

2001-11-02

2001-11-02

"the Term Date and every fifth anniversary thereof" (Term Date = 28 September 1999)

1999-09-28

1999-09-28

Pattern B — First Review Date Must Be Calculated
Use Pattern B when the wording states that reviews occur after an anniversary of a reference date.

Typical wording:

"each fifth anniversary of X"

"every twenty-fifth anniversary of X"

"the fifth anniversary of X and every subsequent fifth year"

In these cases the first review date is not printed.

The first review date can only be obtained by adding a period to another date.

Because this requires date arithmetic, the extractor must not populate anchor_date.

Instead:



"anchor_date": "",
"anchor_kind": "relative_mechanism"
The verbatim wording must be copied into:



"review_date_mechanism"
The reference date itself may still be recorded if it resolves to a printed calendar date.

Where a printed review date aligns with a recognisable interval from another date, use the printed date directly. Do not reclassify the anchor by the inferred relationship. Classify by what the clause says, not by coincidental numerical alignment.

Where review_type = "index_linked" and the first review is the printed index base date (no period added), anchor_date and indexation.base_date carry the same printed value. 13A.10 — Hard rules
Never calculate any date. The extractor records printed dates (directly or by cross-reference) and timing wording only. All date arithmetic — including adding a cycle period to a reference date — is performed by the tool.

A date that needs arithmetic, or is defined by relative/anniversary wording → anchor_date = "", anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism", review_date_mechanism populated.

Pattern A printed reference → anchor_date = cycle_reference_date. Pattern B → anchor_date = "" (never computed).

A relative date is not "unresolvable." Use relative_mechanism and do not raise anchor_unresolved. Reserve unresolvable (and the anchor_unresolved flag) for anchors that cannot be determined at all, even by the tool (e.g. anchored to an undefined future event).

Never guess an anchor date. Genuinely unresolvable → "" and unresolvable, flag anchor_unresolved.

Do not default to lease commencement. Only when the clause explicitly anchors there.

Classify anchor_kind by what the clause says, not by coincidental alignment.

Cross-referencing within the document set is permitted and required for anchor resolution — but only to reach a printed calendar date, never to enable a calculation.

anchor_reasoning must be plain business language. No clause references, no page numbers.

When no anchor_kind fits, use other with a descriptive anchor_label; flag anchor_kind_other.

When anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism" — i.e. the review date, or a cycle's reference date, is defined by relative or anniversary wording — the review_date_mechanism field must record the printed timing wording verbatim from the document. This applies to both single relative dates (trigger.type = "date") and relative cycles (trigger.type = "cycle"), and preserves the source wording for downstream date calculation by the tool layer.

When anchor_kind is anything other than "relative_mechanism" (a printed calendar date, a resolvable event, or no timing found), set review_date_mechanism to "".

All default false. Set true only where expressly printed. Constraints modify outcome — they never create additional records.

19.1 — Evaluation order (Binding)
Constraints must be evaluated BEFORE internal_evidence is trimmed.

Action

1

Read the full review clause, including all sub-paragraphs, settlement paragraphs, determination procedure paragraphs, and cross-referenced definitions.

2

3

4

19.2 — Constraint definitions and recognition patterns
greater_of — Rent expressed as the higher of two figures at the review date.

Patterns

"greater of", "whichever is greater", "shall not be less than", "the higher of (a)… and (b)…"

Trap

Where one limb is the passing rent → also triggers floor_to_previous_rent and upward_only_review. Set all three true.

floor_to_previous_rent — New rent cannot fall below the rent payable immediately before the review.

Patterns

Prior rent appearing as a limb, "not less than the rent payable in the preceding period", express upward-only wording

Trap

Upward-only wording in a separate clause still triggers this.

cap_present — Printed ceiling limits the reviewed rent.

Patterns

"shall not exceed", "subject to a maximum of", indexation capped at X%

Trap

A cap on index movement also populates indexation.cap_percent.

statutory_staging — Statute requires phased implementation.

Patterns

Explicit reference to a statutory provision requiring staged increases

Not triggered by

General references to statutory freeze contingencies without staging wording

interim_rent_pending_determination — Existing rent continues pending determination.

Patterns

"shall continue to be payable", "on account of the Rent subsequently found to be payable", "continues until ascertainment"

Note

"On account" in rent review context means pending determination — set true. The exclusion for quarterly on-account payments applies only to service charge reconciliation.

backdating — Reviewed rent applies retrospectively from the review date with a balancing payment.

Patterns

"apportioned on a daily basis for the period commencing on the Review Date", "due as a debt", "calculated from the relevant Review Date", any wording requiring settlement covering the gap

Trap

Do not require "backdate" or "retrospective" to appear. The substantive effect triggers the constraint.

interest_on_backpay — Interest payable on the balancing sum.

Patterns

"together with interest at [rate]", "with Interest thereon at the Base Rate", interest inline with backdating or in a determination procedure paragraph

19.3 — Scan-the-whole-document rules (Hard)
Scan

What to check

General Interest Clause Scan

Before finalising interest_on_backpay = false, scan the entire document for a general interest clause on unpaid/overdue sums. If found with no carve-out excluding reviewed rent → set true.

Settlement Paragraph Scan

Before finalising backdating = false or interim_rent_pending_determination = false, scan for settlement/determination procedure paragraphs. Typical headings: "Rent Pending Determination", "Settlement", "Review Pending Ascertainment".

Schedule Cross-Reference Scan

If the review clause references a schedule, the constraint scan must include every paragraph of that schedule.

19.4 — Evidence sourcing (Binding)
Each constraint set to true must be supported by wording in at least one internal_evidence.text value.. A true constraint with no supporting segment is a validation failure.

19.5 — Pre-output constraint check
For each false constraint: was the full scan performed? Does any wording match? If yes → correct to true and add the supporting segment.

For each true constraint: is the supporting wording present in internal_evidence? If not → add it.

Status

When

valid

defective_missing_timing

defective_missing_mechanism

defective_missing_both

22. Pattern Recognition Annex
Recognition aids only. The Control Spine governs any conflict.

Triggers: Printed date · Cycle ("every five years") · Constructed ("tenth anniversary of…") · Financial year · Event (Completion / Letting) · Break-linked ("if not exercised… then…")

Mechanisms: Market ("open market rent" / "rental value") · Index-linked (CPI / RPI) · Formula (percentage / multiplier) · Income ("percentage of Turnover" / "Gross Rents" / "Rental Income") · Comparative ("greater of" / "not less than") · Expert / arbitration

Guards: Certification / audit / reconciliation alone ≠ re-determination. Expert → classify by the underlying basis unless expert determination is itself the primary method.

Structures: Multi-phase (separate record per phase) · Cross-schedule (timing/mechanism may sit in a schedule) · Embedded valuation term (own timing inside the definition) · Backdating/statutory (alongside the main mechanism) · OCR (rely on timing + re-determination language, not layout)

Anchor patterns (no date arithmetic — the tool computes):

Single printed date in particulars → anchor is named_review_date or printed_date, resolved by cross-reference.

Multiple discrete printed dates → anchor_date is the earliest printed date in trigger.dates.

Cycle Pattern A ("X and each anniversary") → the first review is X. If X is a printed date, anchor_date = that printed date (a copy, not a calculation).

Cycle Pattern B ("each anniversary of X") → the first review is X + one cycle. That requires arithmetic, so anchor_date = "", anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism", review_date_mechanism = the verbatim wording; record the printed cycle_reference_date and cycle_period and let the tool compute.

Anniversary-defined single date ("the fifth anniversary of X") → trigger.dates = ["RELATIVE"], anchor_date = "", anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism", review_date_mechanism populated.

Cycle anchored to a named event (Development Letting Date, Completion Date) not defined as a date → anchor_kind = "unresolvable".

Indexation base date → if the base date is itself the first review (printed), anchor_date = indexation.base_date; if the first review is the base date plus a period, anchor_date = "" and the tool computes.

23. Output Guarantee
Review signal exists → return { "consideration_review": [ … ] }.

No review signal exists → return either:

{ "consideration_review": [] } — an empty array; or

{ "consideration_review": [ {…} ] } containing a single Negative Check record documenting the search performed.

The Negative Check record is an audit trail proving the document was read for review wording and none was found. Use it for any document type where documenting the search adds downstream value — at the model's discretion.

Negative Check record — structural requirements:

status = "defective_missing_both"

review_type = "unknown"

trigger.type = "unknown"; all other trigger fields empty strings or empty arrays

trigger.anchor_kind = "unresolvable"; all other anchor fields empty strings

reviewed_rent_components = []

review_basis = "", review_effective_date = "", currency = "", review_date_mechanism = ""

indexation = all defaults (empty strings, zeros, false)

constraints = all booleans false

upward_only_review = false

flags = ["missing_or_unresolved_timing", "missing_or_unresolved_mechanism"]

"internal_evidence": [
  {
    "page": 0,
    "clause": "NEGATIVE CHECK",
    "text": "Pages reviewed: [pages]. Terms checked: review, rent review, fee review, increase, adjustment, anniversary, CPI, RPI, index, market, open market. Result: no rent review mechanism found."
  }
]

internal_source = the document type name as printed (e.g. "LEASE", "SITTING-OUT LICENCE", "TENANCY AGREEMENT", "SUPPLEMENTAL DEED")

Terms to check, at minimum: "review", "rent review", "fee review", "increase", "adjustment", "anniversary", "CPI", "RPI", "index", "market", "open market". Add others relevant to the document.

Both outputs (empty array and Negative Check record) are equally valid — neither indicates an extraction failure. No placeholders in other fields. Strict JSON per the response_format schema.
