review_type 

Passes the Review Type Self-Test (Section 7). 

Anchor resolution 

trigger.type = "cycle" or "date" → anchor fields populated or explicitly "" with explanation. 

Pattern A/B 

Correctly applied to cycle triggers. 

review_date_mechanism consistency 

If anchor_kind = "relative_mechanism" → review_date_mechanism must be non-empty. For any other anchor_kind → review_date_mechanism must be "". 

anchor_kind consistency 

If the review date, or a cycle's reference date, is defined by relative or anniversary wording → anchor_kind must be "relative_mechanism" and anchor_date must be "". Applies to both date and cycle triggers. 

No calculated dates 

No date in trigger.dates or anchor_date was derived from anniversary or relative wording. If any was → set anchor_date "", anchor_kind "relative_mechanism", and populate review_date_mechanism; for a date trigger also set trigger.dates ["RELATIVE"]. 

Constraints 

Every true constraint has supporting wording in internal_evidence. 

internal_source 

Document title as printed. Not a placeholder. Same on every record from the same document. 

review_change_type 

One of the permitted values. Not "" on actual records. 

targets_review_description 

Populated on variation/removal. "" on original/addition.

schema.title: Consideration Review Extraction Schema v1.4
schema.description: Schema for extracting rent review mechanisms from property lease documents. Version 1.4 updates cycle fields to be human-readable and keeps internal_evidence as a structured evidence array containing page, clause, and text.

consideration_review.description: Array of review records. Each item represents a single rent review provision.

review_basis.description: Plain-language description of the basis on which rent is reviewed.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Empty string when not printed in the document.

upward_only_review.description: Whether the review can only increase and never decrease the rent.

reviewed_rent_components.description: Which rent components this review applies to.

review_effective_date.description: Calendar date on which the review provision takes effect. Used for phase transitions or delayed-effect variations. Not the same as trigger.anchor_date. Empty string where no distinct from-date is printed.

review_date_mechanism.description: The printed timing wording that defines how the review date is calculated, recorded verbatim from the document. Populated whenever anchor_kind = 'relative timing wording'. This includes review dates or cycle reference dates defined by relative or anniversary wording rather than a printed calendar date. Must be an empty string in all other cases. Never contains a calculated date. The tool layer resolves this into a calendar date using Key Dates.

status.description: Whether the review clause is complete or has defects.

flags.description: Any additional flags or warnings about this review.

review_change_type.description: How this record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_review_description.description: Plain-language description of the original review being varied or removed. Populated only when review_change_type is 'variation' or 'removal'. Empty string on 'original' and 'addition' records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted review provision. Each item represents one source location.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, heading, or other source reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extraction. No commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

dates.description: For date: one or more specific review dates as ISO dates, OR ['RELATIVE'] when the review date is defined by relative or anniversary wording rather than a printed calendar date. For cycle: may contain pre-cycle discrete dates. For event or unknown: typically empty. Never ['UNKNOWN'].

cycle.description: Description of the review cycle. Empty string when not a cycle.

event.description: Description of the triggering event, if applicable. Empty string when not event-triggered.

anchor_date.description: The first review date in the review sequence. Empty string if the first review date is not printed or requires date arithmetic.

anchor_label.description: Verbatim label as used in the clause. Empty string if the clause prints a calendar date directly without a label.

anchor_source.description: Plain-language description of where the anchor date was found or why it cannot be resolved.

enum: cannot be resolved from available documents
anchor_kind.description: Human-readable classification of the date used to anchor the review mechanism.

anchor_reasoning.description: One sentence explaining how the anchor was identified and resolved. No clause numbers or page references.

cycle_reference_date.description: The calendar date referenced by the review cycle wording. This may be different from anchor_date. Empty string if not printed, not applicable, or cannot be resolved.

enum: first review calculated from reference date
cycle_pattern.description: Human-readable classification of how the review cycle operates.

cycle_period.description: Human-readable review interval, for example 'every year', 'every 5 years', or 'every 25 years'. Empty string when not a cycle or when the interval cannot be determined.

cycle_end_date.description: Last review date if a specific end date is printed. Empty string otherwise.

index_name.description: Name of the index. Empty string if not index-linked.

base_date.description: Base date for the indexation mechanism. Empty string if not index-linked or not printed.

cap_percent.description: Maximum percentage increase per review. null if no cap.

floor_percent.description: Minimum percentage increase per review. null if no floor.

upwards_only.description: Whether the indexation can only increase rent.

greater_of.description: Whether the new rent is the greater of two or more calculations.

floor_to_previous_rent.description: Whether the new rent cannot fall below the previous rent.

cap_present.description: Whether a cap on the review outcome is present.

interim_rent_pending_determination.description: Whether interim rent is payable while the review is being determined.

backdating.description: Whether the reviewed rent is backdated to the review date.

interest_on_backpay.description: Whether interest is charged on any backdated rent difference.

fields.name: key_dates
fields.description:
[v3.3] Key Dates — Lifecycle-Only Extraction Prompt
0. Pre-Output Failure Checks — run before returning JSON
Check

Failure

Fix

⚠ A

term_end_date record created but no printed calendar string in the quoted clause is the end date. A duration ("125 years") is not a date. A start date is not an end date.

No record. Duration goes in term_period only.

⚠ B

Notes say "out of scope" or "excluded" but the record exists.

DELETE. A record describing itself as excluded must not exist.

⚠ C

Record captures a "First Rent Commencement Date", "Second Rent Commencement Date", or equivalent. These are financial milestones, not lifecycle dates.

DELETE.

⚠ D

Record captures a break date or break notice date. Out of scope.

DELETE.

⚠ E

date_value does not appear as a printed calendar date in the clause cited in internal_evidence. The date was borrowed from another clause.

DELETE.

1. Purpose
Extract lifecycle key dates only — dates governing the start, continuation, variation, assignment, or end of the right to occupy. Ignore dates relating only to rent, licence fees, reviews, payments, breaks, or obligations.

Return strict JSON per response_format schema. No intermediate steps.

2. Printed-Date Rule
Extract only dates printed in full as day + month + year. All three components must be present.

Never create a date from:

Excluded source

Anniversary rules

Cycles / frequencies

Quarter days without a year

Definitions without a printed date

Calculations (e.g. "10 years from the date of this lease")

Incomplete dates

Statute years

Metadata / filenames

Guesses

Hard rule

Detail

ISO normalisation

Record date_value in YYYY-MM-DD only when unambiguous. Ambiguous numeric dates → date_value = "", note as ambiguous.

No-Date-No-Record

Clause does not print a full calendar date → no record.

No Cross-Clause Date Borrowing (absolute)

date_value must come from the same clause cited in internal_evidence. Clause describes an event but does not print a date → no record. No exceptions.

No Computed Dates (absolute)

A duration is not a printed date. Do not add a duration to a start date to produce an end date. If no end date is printed → no term_end_date record. Duration goes in term_period.

3. This-Document Boundary
Every record must answer: does this date govern THIS document's lifecycle?

3.1 — This document's dates (use specific date_types)
Event

date_type

event_role

Execution

document_date

document_signed

Term start

commencement_date

commencement

Term end

term_end_date

term_end

Effective date

effective_date

effective

Extended expiry

extended_expiry_date

term_extension

Surrender / vacate / termination

Use specific type

Use specific role

3.2 — Other instruments' dates
date_type: "other", event_role: "other". Notes must identify the referenced instrument. Never classify as document_date, commencement_date, or effective_date.

3.3 — Statutory declaration and contracting-out dates
date_type: "other", event_role: "other". These confirm compliance with a statutory process — they do not start, end, or vary occupation.

4. Lifecycle Scope — what to capture
4.1 — Document dates
This document's date of creation, confirmation, variation, extension, assignment, or termination. "Granted pursuant to" / "in pursuance of" an agreement dated X → effective_date. Do not classify an agreement date as commencement_date unless wording expressly states occupation begins on that date.

4.2 — Term and possession dates
Term commencement, term expiry (only if explicitly printed — not computed), extended expiry, contractual possession/handover, vacant possession, vacate date, surrender date. "Commencing on the date hereof" → treat execution date as commencement_date.

4.3 — Completion and trigger dates
Completion of lease/AFL where it activates occupation. Practical completion only when term begins by reference to it. Longstop dates controlling whether the lease continues.

4.4 — Referenced instrument dates
Printed dates of other instruments in definitions, recitals, or schedules → date_type: "other", event_role: "other" per Section 3.2.

5. Out-of-Scope — must be ignored
Even with a full printed date, ignore if it relates only to:

Category

Rent / licence fee payments, rent-free periods, payment-phase boundaries

Rent / licence fee commencement dates (financial milestones, not lifecycle)

Rent / licence fee review dates or review periods

Break dates, break notice dates, break option deadlines

Payment schedules (service charge, insurance, deposit)

Operational / management dates (works, repairs, compliance, accounting)

Statutory / procedural dates not changing occupation rights

Termination triggers contingent on external events without their own printed date

⚠ Out-of-Scope Enforcement Gate (runs before any record is created): Check every candidate against: (a) rent/licence fee commencement or payment boundaries, (b) break dates/notices, (c) review dates/periods, (d) payment schedule dates. If the date relates solely to any of these → do NOT create a record. Record already created and fails this gate → DELETE.

6. date_type and event_role — controlled vocabularies
Never leave either blank. Never free text. First-match-wins. Most specific value.

date_type (first match wins)
Value

commencement_date

term_end_date

extended_expiry_date

vacate_date

surrender_date

termination_date

document_date

execution_date

effective_date

other

event_role (first match wins)
Value

commencement

term_end

term_extension

early_termination

surrender

vacate

document_signed

effective

statutory

other

7. Range Patterns
Two lifecycle dates in a clear range (e.g. "from 1 January 2030 to 31 December 2040"): first → commencement_date / commencement, second → term_end_date / term_end.

Do not apply to rent, payment, review, or break ranges. Do not convert durations to dates.

8. Evidence & Citation [v3.3]
Every date must be supported by internal_evidence.

No evidence → no date record.

internal_source
internal_source must contain the document title as printed.

Use the same document title on every record originating from that document.

Do not include:

Page references

Clause references

Schedule references

Footer references

File names

Solicitor references

Evidence text

Commentary

internal_evidence
internal_evidence is a structured evidence array.

Each evidence object represents a single source reference supporting the extracted date.

Evidence Structure
Each evidence object must contain:



{
  "page": 7,
  "clause": "Particulars",
  "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
}
Field

Description

page

Page number where the evidence appears.

clause

Clause, paragraph, schedule, heading, table row, or section reference. Use an empty string if no reference is printed.

text

Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted date.

Anchor Rule
Every date record must contain at least one anchor evidence object.

The anchor evidence object must contain the printed calendar date supporting date_value.

The anchor evidence object must:

Contain the printed date.

Come from a single page.

Come from a single clause or source reference.

Be verbatim.

Be the shortest wording necessary to support the extracted date.

Support the lifecycle event being extracted.

Examples:



{
  "page": 7,
  "clause": "Particulars",
  "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
}


{
  "page": 42,
  "clause": "Schedule 5",
  "text": "The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
}
Lifecycle Purity Guard
Evidence must support a lifecycle date.

Do not include evidence that relates solely to:

Rent

Licence fees

Rent commencement dates

Rent reviews

Payment dates

Break dates

Break notice deadlines

Service charge dates

Insurance payment dates

Where a clause contains both lifecycle wording and excluded wording, quote only the lifecycle wording.

If this cannot be achieved because of OCR quality, use:



[OCR_UNREADABLE_QUOTE]
Evidence Rules
Each evidence object must:

Represent one source reference.

Be verbatim.

Be relevant to the extracted lifecycle date.

Contain only the wording necessary to support the extraction.

Each evidence object must not:

Contain commentary.

Contain reasoning.

Contain conclusions.

Contain assumptions.

Contain page references inside the text field.

Contain clause references inside the text field.

Contain multiple quotations joined together.

Contain pipe separators (|).

Multiple Supporting Sources
Where multiple source references support the same date, add additional evidence objects.

Correct:



"internal_evidence": [
  {
    "page": 7,
    "clause": "Particulars",
    "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
  },
  {
    "page": 42,
    "clause": "Schedule 5",
    "text": "The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
  }
]
Incorrect:



"internal_evidence": "Page 7 Particulars: Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025 | Page 42 Schedule 5: The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
Emission Gate
Do not emit a record where:

internal_evidence is empty.

No evidence object contains the printed date supporting date_value.

Evidence contains commentary, reasoning, or conclusions.

Evidence contains inferred dates.

Evidence contains computed dates.

Evidence contains multiple source references in a single evidence object.

Evidence contains non-lifecycle wording only.

Evidence contains page references or clause references inside the text field.

Every emitted date record must be traceable back to at least one structured evidence object containing the printed calendar date that supports the extracted value.

9. OCR Tolerance
Condition

Action

Recoverable date (all three components determinable despite garbled characters)

Correct, set date_value, note correction.

Genuinely unrecoverable

Blank.

Date identified in notes

date_value must be populated.

10. date_description
Full operative clause text copied exactly as printed. Include provisos, conditions, alternatives, cross-references, parenthetical wording, all punctuation. No paraphrase. Single line string.

11. Deduplication and Atomic Source
Merge only if ALL match: date_value, date_type, event_role. When multiple source references support the same date record, append additional evidence objects to the internal_evidence array.

Do not combine evidence using "|" separators.

Single-Source Atomic Rule: Same date from same clause → one record only (most specific per first-match-wins). Multiple records for the same date require different clauses with different operative wording.

12. Notes
Short plain-language comment. Examples: "Contractual term start date", "Date of Agreement for Lease referenced in definitions". Do not invent dates. Do not restate clause text. Uncertain → date_type: "other", event_role: "other", explain briefly.

13. Term Object
Always returned alongside key_dates. Populate from printed term details.

Construction rules
Rule

Detail

commencement_date record created

Set term_start_date immediately.

term_end_date record created

Set term_end_date immediately.

Duration explicitly printed

term_period (e.g. "One hundred and fifty years").

is_fixed_term

true only when clearly printed.

Never calculate or infer

Never compute an end date from a period.

No term info

Empty defaults: all fields "", is_fixed_term: false.

⚠ Validity: commencement_date or term_end_date in key_dates must have matching term object field. Mismatch = invalid.

⚠ term_end_date population: Only from a printed calendar date. A duration in term_period does NOT justify computing term_end_date. If no end date is printed → term_end_date = "".

14. Pre-Output Validation
Check

Rule

date_value

ISO YYYY-MM-DD or "" — no computed dates.

date_type and event_role

Populated from vocabularies — never empty.

internal_source

Document title on every record.

internal_evidence

Must be an array.

Every evidence object contains:

page

clause

text

Evidence text must be verbatim.

Evidence text must not contain:

commentary

reasoning

conclusions

page references

clause references

joined quotations using "|"

date_description

Full operative clause verbatim.

Term matching

commencement_date and term_end_date have matching term fields.

No derived dates

No inferred, calculated, or derived dates anywhere.

No nulls

—

Mandatory checks
Check

Rule

This-Document Boundary

date_type other than "other" → must control THIS document's lifecycle. Referenced instrument date → change to "other" / "other".

No-Date-No-Record

At least one internal_evidence object must contain the printed calendar date supporting date_value.

If no evidence object contains the printed date, delete the record. If not → DELETE.

No Cross-Clause Borrowing

date_value must be printed in the cited clause. Borrowed → DELETE.

No Computed Dates

date_value derived from duration + start date → DELETE. term_end_date record without a printed end date → DELETE.

Section 5 Exclusion

Date relates solely to rent/licence fee/review/break/payment → DELETE. Notes saying "out of scope" while record exists = validation failure.

Single-Source

Same date_value + same clause + different date_types → keep most specific only.

internal_source

Must be document title on every record. Page numbers or solicitor codes → correct.

schema.title: Key Dates Extraction Schema v3.3
schema.description: Schema for extracting lifecycle dates from property lease documents. Version 3.3 updates internal_evidence from a free-text string to a structured evidence array.

key_dates.description: Array of individual lifecycle date records extracted from documents in the chain.

date_value.description: The calendar date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Never computed from a duration.

date_type.description: What kind of date this is.

event_role.description: What this date does in the tenancy or document lifecycle.

date_description.description: Verbatim operative clause text. Never paraphrased.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted date. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted date. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Any additional context or observations. Must not contain evidence text, page references, clause references, or structured field values already captured elsewhere.

term.description: Summary of the tenancy term as extracted from the document.

term_period.description: The duration of the term as printed. For example, '10 years'. Empty string if not printed.

term_start_date.description: When the term begins in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Should match the commencement_date record in key_dates where present. Never computed from a duration.

term_end_date.description: When the term is scheduled to end in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Only populated if a full calendar date is printed. Never computed from a duration.

is_fixed_term.description: Whether this is a fixed-term tenancy.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted term summary. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted term. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.description: The effective date of the document

fields.name: description
fields.description: Overall document description that will help find this document on landlord's queries.

fields.name: consideration
fields.description:
[v1.4] Consideration Extraction Prompt — Residential and Licence
Core Assumption (Authoritative)
Input is a residential lease, licence, or licence lease. Extract the occupation rent and licence fee only. Deposits and any costs, charges, or fees other than true rent or licence fee are never extracted.

Return strict JSON only. The output structure is enforced by the schema in response_format. Produce the final JSON directly — no intermediate step.

Permitted consideration_type values: rent and licence fee

If no qualifying rent or licence fee is found after applying all steps → return 

{
  "consideration": [],
  "rent_model_terms": [],
  "contingent_consideration": []
}

Step 0 — Entry Normalisation
Treat each clause, schedule line, or table row as one entry. If an entry contains multiple monetary items, split into components by substance. Process each component independently.

Step 0A — Component Filter (Pre-Signal, Mandatory)
If an entry mentions both rent/licence fee/occupation rent AND any non-rent payment (deposit, security, fees, charges, costs, utilities, services, insurance, etc.) then:

Split the entry

Process only the rent and licence fee component

Ignore all non-rent components entirely (no records, no excluded records)

Instalment Contamination Rule (Critical): If an amount is described as an instalment, balance, remainder, or "remaining X weeks/months" of any deposit or security sum → treat as deposit/security and ignore.

Step 1A — Deposit / Security Kill (Absolute, Terminal)
If the component indicates deposit or security (case-insensitive), including any of the following:

deposit (rent/tenancy/dilapidations/damage deposit)

security / security sum

bond / guarantee / surety

refundable / returnable

held as stakeholder / held as security

instalments/balances/remaining weeks/months of any deposit/security

Then (mandatory): DO NOT construct any record. IGNORE the component. STOP processing this component. No later rule may reintroduce it.

Step 1B — Any Other Cost / Charge Kill (Absolute, Terminal)
If the component indicates any non-rent cost, charge, or fee (case-insensitive), including any of the following:

fee / charge / cost

utilities / bills / council tax / rates

insurance

management / administration

cleaning / inventory / referencing

maintenance / repairs (as payments)

penalties / interest / late payment charges

Then (mandatory): DO NOT construct any record. IGNORE the component. STOP processing this component.

Step 2 — Rent and Licence Fee Substance Test (Authoritative)
Proceed only if the component:

is expressly labelled "rent" or "licence fee"; or

states tenant shall pay rent or licence fee; or

defines rent or licence fee as the occupation payment

Otherwise → ignore.

Step 3 — Payment Mechanics Non-Creation Rule (Terminal)
If a component:

refers to payment of rent or licence fee already defined elsewhere; AND

describes advance payment, lump sum, instalments, or "remaining months/weeks rent" derived from the existing rent or licence fee

Then: DO NOT create a new record. Note the mechanics for optional inclusion in notes of the underlying rent or licence fee record. STOP processing this component.

Step 4 — Rent and Licence Fee Extraction Eligibility (Terminal)
Emit a rent or licence fee record only if:

a printed rent or licence fee amount is stated (digits); OR

an explicit rent or licence fee formula is stated

No inference.

If eligible → proceed to Step 5 (Amount Discipline) before Section 2 (record construction).

If neither is present → proceed to Step 4A.

Step 4A — rent_model_terms Eligibility
Capture a rent_model_terms entry if a rent or licence fee mechanism exists but fails Step 4 (i.e. no fixed digits are printed).

Capture if:

turnover-based rent (percentage of receipts, revenue or turnover)

index-linked rent (RPI, CPI or similar index)

formula-driven rent (calculated by reference to an external variable)

arithmetic progression rent (stepped rent with a printed increment or multiplier)

definition_only mechanisms where a fixed amount is defined but cannot populate consideration

A rent_model_terms record must describe how rent or licence fee is determined.

Do not create rent_model_terms records for:

rent review provisions

open market review mechanisms

surveyor determinations

RICS appointments

review assumptions

review disregards

review dates

deposits

security arrangements

guarantees

service charges

utilities

insurance

penalties

interest provisions

enforcement provisions

any other non-occupation payment mechanism

Step 5 — Amount Discipline (Mandatory — Source-Direct Architecture)
Step 5 governs how payment_amount is determined. The architecture is source-direct: payment_amount is sourced exclusively from the printed digits in the source document. It is NEVER derived from internal_evidence, from any prose construction, from word-form expansion, or from any intermediate text the model produces.

5.0 — Architectural Rule (Read Before Anything Else)
payment_amount and internal_evidence are independent outputs. They are both produced from the same source document, but neither one is derived from the other. The flow is:



source PDF → payment_amount   (mechanical digit copy from printed amount)
source PDF → internal_evidence (verbatim quote, separate operation)
It is NOT:



source PDF → internal_evidence → payment_amount   (FORBIDDEN)
Why this matters. When payment_amount is derived from the model's own quoted text, errors in the quote propagate to the amount. When the model writes prose summaries, it handles amounts like £2,500.00 correctly — but when the same amount has to land in a structured at least one internal_evidence.text value followed by a numeric field, the natural-language generation step corrupts the digits. Welded-symbol amounts like £2250.00 and £2500.00 break repeatedly under this pattern. Decoupling the amount from the quote eliminates the propagation path.

Trust hierarchy. The PRINTED AMOUNT in the source document is ground truth. payment_amount carries that truth directly. internal_evidence is supporting documentation — it is not the source of truth. If internal_evidence and payment_amount ever disagree, payment_amount wins (because it came directly from the printed digits) and internal_evidence is regenerated to match.

5.1 — Source-Direct Amount Sourcing (Mandatory)
For every monetary amount that will populate payment_amount:

(1) Locate the printed amount in the source. Find the exact position of the rent or licence fee figure in the PDF. Identify which characters in the source are the digits of the amount. Examples of what to locate:

In "rent: £2250.00 per calendar month" — the digits are 2 2 5 0 0 0 (six digits total: four before decimal, two after)

In "rent: £2,500.00 per calendar month" — the digits are 2 5 0 0 0 0 (six digits total)

In "The rent is: 2300.00 Monthly" — the digits are 2 3 0 0 0 0 (six digits total)

(2) Count the printed digits. Note the digit count. This count is the gate for correctness. If payment_amount ends up with a different digit count, it is wrong.

(3) Copy the printed digits directly into payment_amount. This is a mechanical transcription operation. You are not generating a number. You are not paraphrasing. You are not reformatting through prose. You are reading the printed digits in the source and copying them, in the same sequence, into the numeric JSON field.

Source shows 2250.00 → payment_amount is 2250 (or 2250.00 if decimal preserved by schema)

Source shows 2500.00 → payment_amount is 2500

Source shows 2300.00 → payment_amount is 2300

Strip the currency symbol if present. Strip thousands separators. Preserve digits and decimal point. Do not strip trailing zeros. Do not round. Do not collapse "00" into nothing.

(4) Self-check. Count the digits in the payment_amount you just wrote. Compare to the count from step (2). The two counts MUST match. If they do not match, the amount has been corrupted — return to step (1) and start over. Do not attempt to patch the amount by editing it.

5.2 — Trailing Zero Trap (Mandatory Final Check)
Before returning the JSON, the following check runs against payment_amount:

Highest-risk pattern. Amounts where the currency symbol is welded directly to the digits with no space (£2250.00, £2500.00, £15000) carry maximum digit-drop risk. Apply this check with extra rigour to any document using welded-symbol amounts.

Defence (mandatory). For every candidate payment_amount:

Generate the variant of the candidate with one extra trailing zero appended before the decimal point (or before end-of-number if no decimal). Candidate 225 → variant 2250. Candidate 250 → variant 2500. Candidate 25 → variant 250.

Search the source for the variant in any format: bare digits ("2250"), with comma ("2,250"), or with currency symbol ("£2250", "£2,250").

Search the source for the candidate as a standalone amount (digit string bounded by non-digit characters on both sides — not a substring of a longer number).

If the variant appears in the source AND the candidate does not appear as a standalone amount → the candidate is wrong. The variant is the correct value. Update payment_amount to the variant. Re-run Step 5.1 from step (1) using the corrected value.

Worked example A. Candidate: 225. Variant: 2250. Source contains "rent: £2250.00 per calendar month". "225" does not appear standalone (only as first three digits of "2250"). Conclusion: candidate wrong. Correct value: 2250.

Worked example B. Candidate: 250. Variant: 2500. Source contains "rent: £2500.00 per calendar month". "250" does not appear standalone. Conclusion: candidate wrong. Correct value: 2500.

Worked example C — recursive. Candidate: 25. First variant: 250. "250" not standalone (only inside "2500"). Apply Trap again. Second variant: 2500. Source contains "rent: £2500.00 per calendar month". Conclusion: candidate wrong by two zeros. Correct value: 2500. The Trap MUST be applied recursively if the first variant also fails to appear standalone.

5.3 — Source-Anchored payment_amount (Constraint)
payment_amount may only take a value that appears as a standalone monetary amount in the source document. "Standalone" means the digit string is bounded by non-digit characters (whitespace, punctuation, currency symbol, or start/end of line) on both sides — it is not a substring of a longer number.

If no standalone occurrence of the candidate digit string exists in the source → the record is invalid. Either correct payment_amount to a value that does occur in the source (via Step 5.2), or DELETE the record.

This is a constraint on what payment_amount is allowed to contain — enforced at generation time. The amount must come from a documented printed figure, not from working memory and not from the model's own quoted text.

5.4 — internal_evidence Generation (Independent of payment_amount)
After payment_amount has been set under Steps 5.1–5.3, generate internal_evidence as a separate output. The quote is sourced directly from the source document, not from payment_amount.

Quote integrity check. After writing internal_evidence, locate the digit string of payment_amount and confirm it appears verbatim within the quote. The check direction is: quote must contain payment_amount, NOT payment_amount must match quote. If the digit string is absent or different in the quote → the quote is wrong (not the amount). Regenerate the quote so it contains the amount verbatim. Never edit payment_amount to match a corrupted quote.

If the quote cannot be made to contain the amount because the amount itself is not actually present in the source as printed → return to Step 5.1 and re-source the amount. The amount is wrong, and the quote was correctly resisting the corruption.

Section 2 — Consideration Record Construction
2.1 — Eligible Rent and Licence Fee Records
Construct one consideration record per eligible finding. consideration_type from the finding. Populate all printed fields:

Field

Rule

payment_amount

Printed numeric digits only (no commas, no currency symbol). Must satisfy Step 5 (Amount Discipline).

currency

ISO 4217 three-letter code if printed; prefer GBP where context supports and currency is not explicitly stated but GBP is clearly implied; otherwise "".

amount_basis

yearly / quarterly / monthly / weekly / daily / hourly / "" (basis wording only when explicitly stated, e.g. "per annum" → yearly).

payment_frequency

yearly / quarterly / monthly / weekly / daily / hourly / "" (how often payment is made, only when explicitly stated).

payment_timing

"in advance" / "in arrears" / "" (advance/arrears wording only when explicitly stated).

payment_dates

Literal printed payment dates text (e.g. "25 March, 24 June"); otherwise "".

payment_start_date

ISO YYYY-MM-DD only if a full calendar date is explicitly printed in the document (e.g. "1 Oct 2024", "from 1 January 2025"). If the start date is expressed as a named date or defined term (e.g. "the Rent Commencement Date"), resolve ONE level: if the definition prints a calendar date → copy it; if the definition contains relative or anniversary wording → leave blank and record start_date_mechanism: in notes with the verbatim wording. If the date requires any calculation (including "the day before X", "X plus 1 month", or any arithmetic) → leave blank and record the mechanism. "" if not printed or not unambiguous.

payment_end_date

ISO YYYY-MM-DD only if a full calendar date is explicitly printed in the document. Same resolution rules as payment_start_date. "The day before X" is a calculation — do not perform it. If the document says "until the date before the Rent Commencement Date" and the Rent Commencement Date is printed as 31 March 2017, do NOT populate "2017-03-30". Leave blank and record end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date in notes. "" if not printed or not unambiguous.

rent_commencement_date

ISO YYYY-MM-DD only if a full calendar date is explicitly printed. Same resolution rules as payment_start_date. "" if not printed or not unambiguous.

2.2 — ISO Date Normalisation (Binding)
Where a date is explicitly printed and unambiguous, convert to ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) for all date fields. If the printed date is numeric or shorthand and could reasonably be interpreted in more than one way (e.g. 5/2/26): treat as ambiguous and output "". No guessing.

2.3 — Date Anti-Calculation Rule (CRITICAL)
The extractor does not perform date arithmetic. All date fields (payment_start_date, payment_end_date, rent_commencement_date) may only contain dates that are printed as full calendar dates in the document. No date may be derived, calculated, or resolved from relative, anniversary, or "day before" wording.

Prohibited operations
Subtracting days (e.g. "the day before 31 March 2017" → do not produce "30 March 2017")

Adding years (e.g. "the second anniversary of 1 October 2024" → do not produce "1 October 2026")

Adding months (e.g. "6 months after the commencement date" → do not calculate)

Any other arithmetic on dates

When a date cannot be populated without calculation
Leave the date field as ""

Record the verbatim wording in notes as start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism:

The tool layer performs the calculation

Mutual exclusivity rule (CRITICAL — zero tolerance)
A populated date field and a mechanism entry in notes for the same date cannot coexist. They are mutually exclusive. The logic is:

If the date is printed → populate the date field. Do NOT record a mechanism.

If the date requires calculation → leave the date field blank. Record the mechanism in notes.

Never do both. If the output contains a populated date field AND a corresponding start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes, the date was calculated. Delete the date. Keep the mechanism.

Self-test before returning JSON: For every record, check:

Condition

Result

payment_start_date is populated AND notes contain start_date_mechanism:

VIOLATION. The date was calculated. Set payment_start_date to "". Keep the mechanism.

payment_end_date is populated AND notes contain end_date_mechanism:

VIOLATION. The date was calculated. Set payment_end_date to "". Keep the mechanism.

payment_start_date is populated AND notes do NOT contain start_date_mechanism:

Correct — date was printed.

payment_start_date is blank AND notes contain start_date_mechanism:

Correct — date requires calculation.

payment_end_date is populated AND notes do NOT contain end_date_mechanism:

Correct — date was printed.

payment_end_date is blank AND notes contain end_date_mechanism:

Correct — date requires calculation.

One-level resolution rule
When the document uses a named date or defined term, resolve to its definition. If the definition contains a printed calendar date → copy it. If the definition contains relative, anniversary, or arithmetic wording → stop. Do not resolve further. Leave blank and capture the mechanism.

Follow defined terms until you reach either a printed calendar date or relative/anniversary wording — whichever you hit first, that determines the outcome. You never calculate past relative wording.

Examples
Resolves to printed date (date field populated, no mechanism):



"From the Rent Commencement Date"
→ Definition: "Rent Commencement Date means 31 March 2017"
→ Printed date found
→ payment_start_date: "2017-03-31"
→ notes: (no start_date_mechanism entry)
Requires calculation — stop (date field blank, mechanism recorded):



"Until the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
→ Rent Commencement Date = 31 March 2017
→ "The date before" requires subtraction → STOP
→ payment_end_date: ""
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
WRONG — both populated (mutual exclusivity violation):



"Until the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
→ payment_end_date: "2017-03-30"  ← CALCULATED — DELETE THIS
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date"  ← KEEP THIS
Corrected:
→ payment_end_date: ""
→ notes: "end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date"
Anniversary wording — stop (date field blank, mechanism recorded):



"From the first anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy"
→ Anniversary wording → STOP
→ payment_start_date: ""
→ notes: "start_date_mechanism: the first anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy"
Chained definition — resolves (date field populated, no mechanism):



"From the Term Start Date"
→ Definition: "Term Start Date means the Rent Commencement Date"
→ Follow one more level: "Rent Commencement Date means 1 October 2024"
→ Printed date found
→ payment_start_date: "2024-10-01"
→ notes: (no start_date_mechanism entry)
Chained definition — stops at relative wording (date field blank, mechanism recorded):



"From the Term Start Date"
→ Definition: "Term Start Date means the first anniversary of the date of this agreement"
→ Anniversary wording → STOP
→ payment_start_date: ""
→ notes: "start_date_mechanism: the first anniversary of the date of this agreement"
Section 3 — Evidence Discipline (Mandatory — All Records)
internal_source
internal_source identifies the document from which the record was extracted.

It must contain the document title as printed on the document.

Examples:



Assured Shorthold Tenancy Agreement
Licence to Occupy
Residential Tenancy Agreement
If no document title is printed, use the source PDF filename.

Do not use:

Transaction identifiers.

Envelope identifiers.

Document fingerprints.

Hash values.

GUIDs.

Page footers.

Clause references.

Page references.

Generic placeholders.

Forbidden examples:



4CC5832F-DD26-4872-983F-25093170190C
LEASE
document
lease_document
DocuSign Envelope ID: XXXXX
Page 3
Clause 4.2
internal_evidence
internal_evidence is supporting documentation.

It is not the source from which payment_amount is derived.

payment_amount and internal_evidence are independent outputs sourced directly from the original document.

The source document is authoritative.

If payment_amount and internal_evidence disagree, the evidence is wrong and must be regenerated. Never alter payment_amount to match a corrupted evidence extract.

internal_evidence is the only field permitted to contain verbatim document wording.

Evidence Structure
internal_evidence must be an array of evidence objects.

Format:



"internal_evidence": [
  {
    "page": 4,
    "clause": "Rent",
    "text": "The Rent shall be £2,500.00 per calendar month"
  }
]
Each evidence object must contain:

Field

Description

page

Page number where the evidence appears.

clause

Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, heading, or similar reference. Use an empty string if no reference exists.

text

Verbatim extract supporting the record.

Evidence Rules
Each evidence object must:

Represent exactly one source location.

Come from exactly one page.

Come from exactly one clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or source reference.

Contain verbatim source wording.

Be the shortest extract sufficient to support the extraction.

Evidence text must:

Preserve all printed digits exactly.

Preserve commas exactly.

Preserve decimal places exactly.

Preserve trailing zeros exactly.

Evidence text must not contain:

Commentary.

Summaries.

Interpretations.

Reasoning.

Assumptions.

Page references.

Clause references.

Ellipses.

Generated wording.

Multiple evidence locations must be represented as multiple evidence objects.

Never concatenate evidence using:

Amount Support Rule
At least one evidence object must contain the printed digit string supporting payment_amount.

Example:

If:



"payment_amount": 2500
then at least one evidence object's text value must contain:



2500
exactly as printed in the source document.

If the digit string supporting payment_amount does not appear in any evidence object:

The evidence is wrong.

Regenerate the evidence.

Do not modify payment_amount.

Evidence Completeness Rule
Evidence must support the fields being extracted.

Where available, evidence should support:

The payment obligation.

The payment amount.

The payment frequency.

The payment timing.

Any conditional wording.

Any printed commencement date.

Use the minimum number of evidence objects necessary.

notes
notes contains supplementary extraction information only.

Use plain business language.

Do not include:

Verbatim lease wording.

Clause references.

Page references.

Evidence text.

Internal prompt terminology.

Advance-payment mechanics identified during Step 3 may be recorded in notes relating to the underlying rent or licence fee record.

Date Mechanism Entries
The following entries are permitted in notes:



start_date_mechanism:
end_date_mechanism:
These entries are only permitted when the corresponding date field is blank.

Rules:

Must contain verbatim wording describing how the date is defined.

Must not contain calculated dates.

Must not contain inferred dates.

Must not contain arithmetic results.

Examples:



start_date_mechanism: the first anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy


end_date_mechanism: the date before the Rent Commencement Date
Notes Ordering Rule
Where present, entries must appear in the following order:

start_date_mechanism:

end_date_mechanism:

All other notes content

Mechanism entries must always appear before any other note content.

Section 4 — rent_model_terms Construction
Construct one rent_model_terms entry per eligible Step 4A finding.

Field

Rule

term_type

definition_only / formula / percentage / turnover_based / indexation / arithmetic_progression

mechanism_summary

Plain description of how the rent is calculated or defined. No lease wording; business language only

requires_external_inputs

true if the mechanism cannot be resolved without external data (e.g. turnover figures, index values)

external_inputs

Array of strings listing required external inputs (e.g. ["RPI figure at review date"]); [] if none

fixed_amount_printed

The fixed amount printed in the source clause if any; "" if no fixed digits appear. Subject to Step 5 (Amount Discipline) if populated

currency

ISO 4217 three-letter code if applicable; "" if not applicable

internal_source

Filename only

internal_evidence

internal_evidence

Same structured evidence format as Section 3.

Each evidence object must contain:

page

clause

text

Multiple evidence locations must be represented as multiple evidence objects.

Never concatenate evidence using "|" separators.

notes

Supporting context only; no lease wording

Do NOT capture: deposit or security mechanisms, service charges, utilities, insurance, rent review mechanisms (surveyor determination, open market review, RICS appointment), interest, penalties, or enforcement provisions.

Section 5 — Review Exclusion Rule (Hard)
Rent review mechanisms are outside the scope of this prompt. Review clauses, review dates, open market reassessment, or surveyor determination: must not populate rent_model_terms. Review language must not cause deletion of an otherwise valid FIXED consideration record.

Section 6 — Pre-Output Validation
Before returning JSON, confirm:

Check

Rule

consideration_type

Only from permitted values: rent / ground rent / turnover rent / peppercorn rent / premium.

payment_amount

Numeric (number type) or "" — no commas, no symbols.

Amount Discipline compliance

(a) payment_amount sourced directly from printed digits, NOT derived from internal_evidence or prose (Steps 5.0–5.1); (b) digit count matches printed amount (Step 5.1 step 4); (c) digit string appears as standalone amount in source (Step 5.3); (d) Trailing Zero Trap applied (Step 5.2). Any failure → DELETE the record.

Quote-Amount direction check

Digit string of payment_amount must appear verbatim within at least one internal_evidence.text value If not → regenerate quote. NEVER edit payment_amount to match a corrupted quote.

Quote verbatim compliance

Text between quotation marks in internal_evidence is exact character-for-character copy of source wording, including every digit, comma, decimal point, and trailing zero. Digit count between source amount and quoted amount must match. Any failure → DELETE the record.

currency

ISO 4217 three-letter code or "".

All date fields

ISO YYYY-MM-DD or "" — no numeric shorthand dates, no ambiguous dates.

Date anti-calculation compliance

No date in payment_start_date, payment_end_date, or rent_commencement_date was derived from relative, anniversary, ordinal, or "day before" wording. If any was → blank the field and populate the corresponding start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes. See Section 2.3.

Mechanism capture completeness

Every blank payment_start_date or payment_end_date has a corresponding start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes explaining how the date is defined in the document. Blank date with no mechanism → investigate.

Mechanism content check

No start_date_mechanism: or end_date_mechanism: entry in notes contains a calculated calendar date. These entries must contain verbatim document wording only — never a resolved date.

internal_source

The document's title as it appears at the top of the first page. Not a filename placeholder, not a transaction ID, not a generic word. No clause or page references.

internal_evidence

internal_evidence

Must be an array.

Every evidence object must contain:

page

clause

text

Evidence text must be verbatim.

Evidence text must not contain:

commentary

reasoning

summaries

interpretations

page references

clause references

"|" separators

Multiple evidence locations must be represented as multiple evidence objects.

At least one internal_evidence.text value must contain the printed digit string supporting payment_amount.

notes

No lease wording, no clause references. start_date_mechanism: and end_date_mechanism: entries are permitted when the corresponding date field is blank. These must contain verbatim document wording, never calculated dates. Mechanism entries appear before any other notes content.

rent_model_terms

rent_model_terms

Must contain only mechanisms that determine the amount of rent or licence fee.

For every rent_model_terms record:

mechanism_summary must be populated.

requires_external_inputs = true must contain at least one corresponding value in external_inputs.

internal_evidence must support the mechanism being extracted.

the mechanism must relate directly to the calculation or determination of rent or licence fee.

Includes:

turnover-based rent

index-linked rent

formula-driven rent

arithmetic progression rent

definition_only mechanisms

Excludes:

deposits

security arrangements

bonds

guarantees

sureties

service charges

utilities

insurance

administration charges

penalties

interest provisions

enforcement provisions

rent review mechanisms

open market reviews

surveyor determinations

RICS appointments

review assumptions

review disregards

review dates

any other non-occupation payment mechanism

If a mechanism does not determine the amount of rent or licence fee, remove it.

No null values

Use "" for strings, [] for arrays, false for booleans.

No extra keys

additionalProperties: false throughout.

If no qualifying rent or licence fee found → consideration: [], rent_model_terms: [].

schema.title: Consideration Extraction Schema [v1.4]
schema.description: Schema for extracting lease and licence consideration data from property documents. Version 1.4 separates fixed consideration, rent and licence fee model terms, and contingent consideration mechanisms such as overage, clawback, uplift and profit share.

consideration.description: Array of fixed payment obligation records extracted from the lease or licence. Use this for fixed rent, ground rent, base rent, peppercorn rent, premium, reverse premium, landlord contribution and licence fee records only.

consideration_type.description: The category of fixed payment. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

payment_amount.description: The monetary amount. 0 is permitted for peppercorn rent only. All others must be greater than 0. Landlord-to-tenant payments are recorded as positive amounts.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, for example GBP. Peppercorn rent must be empty string. No currency symbols.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before this fixed payment obligation takes effect. Empty string when unconditional.

amount_basis.description: The basis on which the amount is expressed.

payment_frequency.description: How often payment is made. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where no recurring frequency applies.

payment_timing.description: Whether payment is made in advance or arrears. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where neither applies.

payment_dates.description: Specific dates on which payment falls due. Free text, verbatim from the lease where printed.

payment_start_date.description: Start date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

payment_end_date.description: End date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

rent_commencement_date.description: The date rent or licence fee payments begin. ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

consideration_change_type.description: How this record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original record being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the fixed payment obligation.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted payment obligation. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

rent_model_terms.description: Array of occupation payment adjustment or calculation mechanism records. Use this for rent or licence fee mechanisms only. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

term_type.description: Classification of the rent or licence fee mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the rent or licence fee mechanism works. Must begin with: HIGHER_OF, TOP_UP, FIXED_PLUS_VARIABLE, VALUATION_BASED, or STANDALONE.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Starting amount for arithmetic_progression, or the fixed amount printed in the source clause. Empty string for all other term types or when no fixed digits appear.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism depends on external data, such as index values or turnover figures.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs needed. Empty array if none required.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the rent or licence fee mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

contingent_consideration.description: Array of contingent, deferred, variable or event-triggered consideration payment mechanisms which are not fixed occupation rent and are not rent calculation mechanisms. Use this for overage, clawback, uplift payments, profit share, development gain, planning gain, additional consideration, further consideration and similar landlord-benefiting contingent payments.

consideration_type.description: Use only for contingent consideration obligations that are not fixed rent, premium, licence fee or landlord contribution.

mechanism.description: The specific type of contingent consideration mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the contingent payment is calculated or triggered. Do not calculate the payment.

trigger_event.description: Plain-language description of the event that triggers the payment obligation. Empty string if not clear.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string if no currency is printed.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Any fixed monetary amount printed as part of the contingent mechanism. Empty string if none.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism requires external information to determine the amount payable, such as sale price, profit, valuation, development cost, planning uplift, lettings income or disposal proceeds.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs required to calculate the contingent payment. Empty array if none.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before the contingent payment obligation applies. Empty string when unconditional.

consideration_change_type.description: How this contingent consideration record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original contingent consideration being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the contingent consideration mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted contingent consideration mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

fields.name: consideration_review
fields.description:
[v1.2] Consideration Review — Extraction Prompt (Residential & Licence of Occupation)
Overview
Extract rent re-determination mechanisms from residential leases and licences to occupy — when and how rent can change during the term. Fixed sums, peppercorn rents, premiums, and service fees belong in the consideration prompt. No duplication between prompts.

For each mechanism, capture both (a) the mechanism as it appears in the document and (b) the resolved anchor date plus cycle interval so downstream systems can compute the next review date without further document reading — except where the review date is defined by relative or anniversary wording, in which case the extractor captures the verbatim mechanism wording and leaves date calculation to the tool layer.

Return strict JSON only per response_format schema. No intermediate steps. Do not infer, calculate, or reconstruct — except where Section 6 (Anchor Resolution) explicitly permits cross-referencing to a printed calendar date.

0. Pre-Output Failure Checks — Run Before Returning JSON
Before returning JSON, execute every check in Section 12 (Self-Check). The highest-risk failures are:

Check

Failure

Fix

A

Collapsed multi-component structure: rent structure contains N distinct mechanisms at different cadences but output has fewer than N records

Must contain N separate records

B

Conflated reviewed_rent_components: a record lists components belonging to other mechanisms

Each record's reviewed_rent_components must name only the component(s) that record's mechanism re-determines

C

Over-quoted evidence: internal_evidence exceeds 5 segments

Must contain only the anchor + minimum supporting segments for timing, mechanism, and constraints

D

Misclassified review_type: index mechanism classified as market, or market classified as index

Index (RPI/CPI) → index_linked. Open market rental value → market. Fixed step → determinative. Apply the Review Type Self-Test (Section 4)

E

Missing anchor resolution: trigger.type = cycle or date but anchor fields blank without explanation

Every such record must have anchor_date and anchor_kind populated (or explicitly "" and "unresolvable" — or "" and "relative_mechanism" when the date is defined by anniversary/relative wording)

F

Wrong pattern applied to cycle anchor: Pattern A/B mismatch

"X and each anniversary" → Pattern A, anchor_date = cycle_reference_date. "Each anniversary of X" → Pattern B, anchor_date = cycle_reference_date + cycle_period

G

Relative date calculated: trigger.dates contains a calendar date that was derived from anniversary or relative wording rather than printed in the document

trigger.dates must be ["RELATIVE"]. review_date_mechanism must contain the verbatim timing wording. anchor_date must be "". anchor_kind must be "relative_mechanism". The extractor does not perform date arithmetic.

1. Qualification Gate
Before creating any record: does this document contain a re-determination mechanism?

Condition

Action

Rent fixed at a nominal or specified sum with no review wording

Do not extract

Phase end date alone (no review trigger)

Do not extract

Mechanism that sets or recalculates rent at a trigger

Proceed

Clause prints both a fixed phase and a subsequent mechanism

Apply the gate to each phase independently

Recall-first rule: re-determination mechanism present → create a record. Defects affect field population only — never suppress the record.

1.1 Qualifying Mechanism Test
Both conditions must be true from printed wording:

Triggered — fires at a printed date, cycle, or event

Determinative — sets or recalculates rent by valuation, formula, index, or fixed rule

If either is absent → does not qualify.

1.2 Exclusion Table
Excluded mechanism

Unless

Periodic payment timing

(absolute)

Reporting or certification

Process itself determines rent for that period

Service charge reconciliation

Resets rent at a printed trigger

Break-condition rent changes

(absolute)

Fixed nominal/peppercorn phases

(absolute)

Pre-agreed fixed step (both amounts printed, no valuation/formula/index)

Re-determination wording expressly present

Fixed Step Exclusion (absolute): Step from one printed fixed amount to another on a printed date = pre-agreed obligation, not re-determination. Test: are both amounts printed fixed sums with no valuation, formula, index, or expert? If yes → excluded.

2. Review Signal Gate
An entry passes if it contains printed wording signalling rent re-determination at a trigger.

Category

Signal

A. Express review

review / reviewed / re-determined / revised; Review Date / Rent Review

B1. Cycle signals (recurring → type cycle)

every X years / each anniversary / annual review / X and each anniversary thereof

B2. Single-date anniversary (non-recurring → type date)

the Xth anniversary of Y (with no "and each" continuation) / on the Review Date (singular)

C. Market valuation

open market / market rent / rental value

D. Indexation

CPI / RPI / indexation / index-linked adjustment

E. Expert/arbitration

expert / surveyor / arbitrator — where the appointee fixes rent

F. Formula/percentage

formula / percentage / multiplier wording resetting rent at a trigger

G. Fixed step with review wording

£X up to… and £Y from and including [date] — where re-determination language is present

Linguistic test: definite article + ordinal = singular. "The fifth anniversary" = one date. "Each fifth anniversary" = a series.

Contamination guard: entry containing only notice mechanics, payment timing, or procedural steps — without trigger-based re-determination wording → no record.

3. Composite Structures
Structure

Rule

Composite greater-of

Rent as the greater of two separately calculated figures → extract a separate record for each mechanism. Set greater_of = true on both. Never collapse into one record

Multiple components at different cadences

One record per component per mechanism. Different cadence = different trigger. Never merge an annual indexation with a periodic market review

Cycle timing wording

Copy timing wording only into trigger.cycle. Purpose/operative statements belong in review_basis only

Classify from printed mechanism wording only. Once set, final.

Use when

comparative

Is the primary driver wrapped in a comparative structure where one limb is the passing rent or a fixed floor? → the wrapper is a constraint, not the review type.

5.3 Ordinal Locators vs Interval Wording
Type

Example

Meaning

Ordinal locator (one date)

"the fifth anniversary of X"

A position on a timeline. Points to one date. Does not establish recurrence

Interval wording (cycle)

"every fifth anniversary of X", "each anniversary of X"

A length between reviews. Creates a series

Hard rule: ordinal locator standing alone → trigger.type = date. Do not populate cycle_period or cycle_reference_date. Set cycle_pattern = "not_applicable".

Grammatical test: definite article ("the") + ordinal + singular "anniversary" = one date. "Every", "each", or plural "anniversaries" = cycle.

One-level resolution rule (CRITICAL — added in v1.1)
When resolving an ordinal locator or any named review date term, resolve the definition ONE level only.

If the definition contains relative or anniversary wording (e.g. "the fifth anniversary of the Term Commencement Date"), stop. Do not look up the Term Commencement Date. Do not calculate a calendar date. The definition itself determines the classification.

Result:

trigger.dates: ["RELATIVE"]

trigger.type: "date"

review_date_mechanism: populated with the verbatim definition wording

anchor_date: ""

anchor_kind: "relative_mechanism"

Example of the prohibited chain:



"Review Date" → definition says "the annual anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy"
→ AI looks up commencement date → finds "1 October 2024"
→ AI calculates 1 October 2024 + 1 year = 1 October 2025
→ AI puts "2025-10-01" in trigger.dates
This is WRONG. The definition contains anniversary wording. Stop at the definition. Classify as relative.

Correct handling:



"Review Date" → definition says "the annual anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy"
→ Definition contains anniversary wording → STOP
→ trigger.dates: ["RELATIVE"]
→ trigger.type: "date"
→ review_date_mechanism: "the annual anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy"
→ anchor_date: ""
→ anchor_kind: "relative_mechanism"

What the definition contains

Classification

Result

A full calendar date printed in the definition itself (e.g. "Review Date means 1 October 2025")

Printed date

Relative or anniversary wording (e.g. "the annual anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy")

Relative

A reference to another defined term that itself contains a calendar date

Printed date (the final definition prints it)

A reference to another defined term that itself contains relative or anniversary wording

The rule in one sentence: Follow defined terms until you reach either a printed calendar date or relative/anniversary wording — whichever you hit first, that determines the classification. You never reach past relative wording to calculate a date.

Populate only if the clause prints a specific "from" date on which the review provision takes effect. This is not the same as anchor_date. Where no distinct "from" date is printed, leave blank.

6. Anchor Resolution
Every trigger of type cycle or date must populate the anchor fields. Triggers of type event populate where the event's calendar date is resolvable. Triggers of type unknown leave anchor fields blank.

6.0 Anti-calculation gate (CRITICAL — added in v1.1)
Before applying any resolution pathway in 6.2: check whether the review date is defined by relative or anniversary wording (as determined in Section 5.3).

If yes → do NOT resolve further. Set:

anchor_date: ""

anchor_kind: "relative_mechanism"

anchor_reasoning: plain-language description of why the date cannot be resolved (e.g. "Review date defined as the annual anniversary of the commencement of the tenancy — anniversary wording, not a printed calendar date")

review_date_mechanism: verbatim timing wording

The resolution pathways in 6.2 apply ONLY when the review date is a printed calendar date or when cross-referencing leads to a printed calendar date — never when it leads to relative or anniversary wording.

6.1 Cycle Pattern Classification
The extractor never performs date arithmetic.

The extractor may:

copy a printed calendar date

look up a printed calendar date from a defined term

record review timing wording exactly as written

The extractor must never:

add years

add months

add days

calculate anniversaries

derive review dates from relative wording

All date calculations are performed by the tool layer.

anchor_date
anchor_date is populated only when the first review date is a printed calendar date or can be obtained by looking up a printed calendar date elsewhere in the document.

If determining the first review date requires any calculation, anniversary logic, or date arithmetic:



{
  "anchor_date": ""
}
and the review timing wording must be recorded in:



{
  "review_date_mechanism": ""
}
for downstream resolution by the tool.

Cycle Pattern Types
Anchor Date Is First Review
The clause identifies a date which is itself the first review date.

Typical wording:

"X and each anniversary thereafter"

"X and every fifth anniversary thereafter"

"The Review Date being X and each subsequent fifth anniversary"

Examples:

Clause wording

cycle_reference_date

anchor_date

1 April 2020 and each anniversary thereafter

2020-04-01

2020-04-01

28 September 1999 and every fifth anniversary thereafter

1999-09-28

1999-09-28

Output:



{
  "cycle_pattern": "anchor date is first review"
}
Reason:

No date calculation is required.

The printed reference date is already the first review date.

First Review Calculated From Reference Date
The clause identifies a reference date, but the first review occurs after one review interval has elapsed.

Typical wording:

"each anniversary of X"

"every fifth anniversary of X"

"the fifth anniversary of X and every subsequent fifth year"

Examples:

Clause wording

cycle_reference_date

anchor_date

each anniversary of the Term Commencement Date (TCD = 1 January 2023)

2023-01-01

""

every fifth anniversary of the Term Commencement Date (TCD = 18 September 1998)

1998-09-18

""

Output:



{
  "cycle_pattern": "first review calculated from reference date"
}
Reason:

The first review date can only be reached by adding a period to the reference date.

The extractor must not perform that calculation.

The tool layer calculates the first review date later.

Relative Date Rule
The cycle classifications above apply only when the reference date itself is a printed calendar date.

If the reference date is defined by anniversary wording, relative wording, or another calculated mechanism:

do not calculate

do not populate anchor_date

set anchor_kind to "relative timing wording"

populate review_date_mechanism with the verbatim timing wording

Example:

Clause:

"The Review Date shall be every fifth anniversary of the Commencement Date."

Where the Commencement Date definition itself contains anniversary wording.

Output:



{
  "anchor_date": "",
  "anchor_kind": "relative timing wording"
}
The tool layer performs the calculation.

each anniversary of X

every fifth anniversary of X

the fifth anniversary of X and every subsequent fifth year

first review calculated from reference date

the fifth anniversary of X

not applicable

Wording such as:

"the fifth anniversary of X"

"the tenth anniversary of X"

without continuation wording creates a single review date only.

These are not recurring review cycles.

Rule

Never guess an anchor date. Unresolvable → "" and "unresolvable". Flag anchor_unresolved.

7. Field Rules
7.1 reviewed_rent_components
Populate with the printed label of the component being re-determined. No label printed → [] permitted. Before returning [], scan the anchor clause and operative rent clause for any defined or quoted label.

7.2 review_basis
Plain language description of how rent is determined at the trigger. No verbatim clause text. No ellipses, no clause references. Business terms: what is valued, how constrained, what happens pending determination.

7.3 Currency
ISO 4217 three-letter code (e.g. "GBP"). Never symbols. Not printed → "".

7.4 Indexation
Index-linked → populate from printed wording. Not index-linked → empty defaults: index_name = "", base_date = "", cap_percent = 0, floor_percent = 0, upwards_only = false.

7.5 review_date_mechanism (structured field — new in v1.1)
When trigger.dates contains ["RELATIVE"], the review_date_mechanism field must record the printed timing wording from the document. This preserves the source wording for downstream date calculation by the tool layer.

When trigger.dates does NOT contain ["RELATIVE"] (i.e. contains ISO dates or is empty), set review_date_mechanism to "".

Mandatory when relative

No calculated dates

9. Constraints
All default false. Set true only where expressly printed. Constraints modify outcome — they never create additional records.

9.1 Evaluation Order
Step

Action

1

Read the full review clause, including all sub-paragraphs and cross-referenced definitions

2

Evaluate each constraint against the full text

3

For each true constraint, retain the supporting wording in internal_evidence

4

Only after steps 1–3, trim surplus segments

9.2 Constraint Definitions
greater_of — Rent expressed as the higher of two figures at the review date.

Patterns: "greater of", "whichever is greater", "shall not be less than", "the higher of (a)… and (b)…"

Trap: where one limb is the passing rent → also triggers floor_to_previous_rent and upward_only_review. Set all three true.

floor_to_previous_rent — New rent cannot fall below the rent payable immediately before the review.

Patterns: prior rent appearing as a limb, "not less than the rent payable in the preceding period", express upward-only wording.

cap_present — Printed ceiling limits the reviewed rent.

Patterns: "shall not exceed", "subject to a maximum of", indexation capped at X%.

Trap: a cap on index movement also populates indexation.cap_percent.

interim_rent_pending_determination — Existing rent continues pending determination.

Patterns: "shall continue to be payable", "on account of the Rent subsequently found to be payable", "continues until ascertainment".

backdating — Reviewed rent applies retrospectively from the review date with a balancing payment.

Patterns: "apportioned on a daily basis for the period commencing on the Review Date", "due as a debt", "calculated from the relevant Review Date".

Trap: do not require "backdate" or "retrospective" to appear. The substantive effect triggers the constraint.

interest_on_backpay — Interest payable on the balancing sum.

Patterns: "together with interest at [rate]", "with Interest thereon at the Base Rate".

What to check

Before finalising interest_on_backpay = false, scan the document for a general interest clause on unpaid sums. If found with no carve-out excluding reviewed rent → set true

Settlement paragraph

Before finalising backdating = false or interim_rent_pending_determination = false, scan for settlement/determination procedure paragraphs

}
