schema.title: Consideration Review Extraction Schema v1.4
schema.description: Schema for extracting rent review mechanisms from property lease documents. Version 1.4 updates cycle fields to be human-readable and keeps internal_evidence as a structured evidence array containing page, clause, and text.

consideration_review.description: Array of review records. Each item represents a single rent review provision.

review_basis.description: Plain-language description of the basis on which rent is reviewed.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Empty string when not printed in the document.

upward_only_review.description: Whether the review can only increase and never decrease the rent.

reviewed_rent_components.description: Which rent components this review applies to.

review_effective_date.description: Calendar date on which the review provision takes effect. Used for phase transitions or delayed-effect variations. Not the same as trigger.anchor_date. Empty string where no distinct from-date is printed.

review_date_mechanism.description: The printed timing wording that defines how the review date is calculated, recorded verbatim from the document. Populated whenever anchor_kind = 'relative timing wording'. This includes review dates or cycle reference dates defined by relative or anniversary wording rather than a printed calendar date. Must be an empty string in all other cases. Never contains a calculated date. The tool layer resolves this into a calendar date using Key Dates.

status.description: Whether the review clause is complete or has defects.

flags.description: Any additional flags or warnings about this review.

review_change_type.description: How this record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_review_description.description: Plain-language description of the original review being varied or removed. Populated only when review_change_type is 'variation' or 'removal'. Empty string on 'original' and 'addition' records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted review provision. Each item represents one source location.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, heading, or other source reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extraction. No commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

dates.description: For date: one or more specific review dates as ISO dates, OR ['RELATIVE'] when the review date is defined by relative or anniversary wording rather than a printed calendar date. For cycle: may contain pre-cycle discrete dates. For event or unknown: typically empty. Never ['UNKNOWN'].

cycle.description: Description of the review cycle. Empty string when not a cycle.

event.description: Description of the triggering event, if applicable. Empty string when not event-triggered.

anchor_date.description: The first review date in the review sequence. Empty string if the first review date is not printed or requires date arithmetic.

anchor_label.description: Verbatim label as used in the clause. Empty string if the clause prints a calendar date directly without a label.

anchor_source.description: Plain-language description of where the anchor date was found or why it cannot be resolved.

enum: cannot be resolved from available documents
anchor_kind.description: Human-readable classification of the date used to anchor the review mechanism.

anchor_reasoning.description: One sentence explaining how the anchor was identified and resolved. No clause numbers or page references.

cycle_reference_date.description: The calendar date referenced by the review cycle wording. This may be different from anchor_date. Empty string if not printed, not applicable, or cannot be resolved.

enum: first review calculated from reference date
cycle_pattern.description: Human-readable classification of how the review cycle operates.

cycle_period.description: Human-readable review interval, for example 'every year', 'every 5 years', or 'every 25 years'. Empty string when not a cycle or when the interval cannot be determined.

cycle_end_date.description: Last review date if a specific end date is printed. Empty string otherwise.

index_name.description: Name of the index. Empty string if not index-linked.

base_date.description: Base date for the indexation mechanism. Empty string if not index-linked or not printed.

cap_percent.description: Maximum percentage increase per review. null if no cap.

floor_percent.description: Minimum percentage increase per review. null if no floor.

upwards_only.description: Whether the indexation can only increase rent.

greater_of.description: Whether the new rent is the greater of two or more calculations.

floor_to_previous_rent.description: Whether the new rent cannot fall below the previous rent.

cap_present.description: Whether a cap on the review outcome is present.

interim_rent_pending_determination.description: Whether interim rent is payable while the review is being determined.

backdating.description: Whether the reviewed rent is backdated to the review date.

interest_on_backpay.description: Whether interest is charged on any backdated rent difference.

fields.name: key_dates
fields.description:
[v3.3] Key Dates — Lifecycle-Only Extraction Prompt
0. Pre-Output Failure Checks — run before returning JSON
Check

Failure

Fix

⚠ A

term_end_date record created but no printed calendar string in the quoted clause is the end date. A duration ("125 years") is not a date. A start date is not an end date.

No record. Duration goes in term_period only.

⚠ B

Notes say "out of scope" or "excluded" but the record exists.

DELETE. A record describing itself as excluded must not exist.

⚠ C

Record captures a "First Rent Commencement Date", "Second Rent Commencement Date", or equivalent. These are financial milestones, not lifecycle dates.

DELETE.

⚠ D

Record captures a break date or break notice date. Out of scope.

DELETE.

⚠ E

date_value does not appear as a printed calendar date in the clause cited in internal_evidence. The date was borrowed from another clause.

DELETE.

1. Purpose
Extract lifecycle key dates only — dates governing the start, continuation, variation, assignment, or end of the right to occupy. Ignore dates relating only to rent, licence fees, reviews, payments, breaks, or obligations.

Return strict JSON per response_format schema. No intermediate steps.

2. Printed-Date Rule
Extract only dates printed in full as day + month + year. All three components must be present.

Never create a date from:

Excluded source

Anniversary rules

Cycles / frequencies

Quarter days without a year

Definitions without a printed date

Calculations (e.g. "10 years from the date of this lease")

Incomplete dates

Statute years

Metadata / filenames

Guesses

Hard rule

Detail

ISO normalisation

Record date_value in YYYY-MM-DD only when unambiguous. Ambiguous numeric dates → date_value = "", note as ambiguous.

No-Date-No-Record

Clause does not print a full calendar date → no record.

No Cross-Clause Date Borrowing (absolute)

date_value must come from the same clause cited in internal_evidence. Clause describes an event but does not print a date → no record. No exceptions.

No Computed Dates (absolute)

A duration is not a printed date. Do not add a duration to a start date to produce an end date. If no end date is printed → no term_end_date record. Duration goes in term_period.

3. This-Document Boundary
Every record must answer: does this date govern THIS document's lifecycle?

3.1 — This document's dates (use specific date_types)
Event

date_type

event_role

Execution

document_date

document_signed

Term start

commencement_date

commencement

Term end

term_end_date

term_end

Effective date

effective_date

effective

Extended expiry

extended_expiry_date

term_extension

Surrender / vacate / termination

Use specific type

Use specific role

3.2 — Other instruments' dates
date_type: "other", event_role: "other". Notes must identify the referenced instrument. Never classify as document_date, commencement_date, or effective_date.

3.3 — Statutory declaration and contracting-out dates
date_type: "other", event_role: "other". These confirm compliance with a statutory process — they do not start, end, or vary occupation.

4. Lifecycle Scope — what to capture
4.1 — Document dates
This document's date of creation, confirmation, variation, extension, assignment, or termination. "Granted pursuant to" / "in pursuance of" an agreement dated X → effective_date. Do not classify an agreement date as commencement_date unless wording expressly states occupation begins on that date.

4.2 — Term and possession dates
Term commencement, term expiry (only if explicitly printed — not computed), extended expiry, contractual possession/handover, vacant possession, vacate date, surrender date. "Commencing on the date hereof" → treat execution date as commencement_date.

4.3 — Completion and trigger dates
Completion of lease/AFL where it activates occupation. Practical completion only when term begins by reference to it. Longstop dates controlling whether the lease continues.

4.4 — Referenced instrument dates
Printed dates of other instruments in definitions, recitals, or schedules → date_type: "other", event_role: "other" per Section 3.2.

5. Out-of-Scope — must be ignored
Even with a full printed date, ignore if it relates only to:

Category

Rent / licence fee payments, rent-free periods, payment-phase boundaries

Rent / licence fee commencement dates (financial milestones, not lifecycle)

Rent / licence fee review dates or review periods

Break dates, break notice dates, break option deadlines

Payment schedules (service charge, insurance, deposit)

Operational / management dates (works, repairs, compliance, accounting)

Statutory / procedural dates not changing occupation rights

Termination triggers contingent on external events without their own printed date

⚠ Out-of-Scope Enforcement Gate (runs before any record is created): Check every candidate against: (a) rent/licence fee commencement or payment boundaries, (b) break dates/notices, (c) review dates/periods, (d) payment schedule dates. If the date relates solely to any of these → do NOT create a record. Record already created and fails this gate → DELETE.

6. date_type and event_role — controlled vocabularies
Never leave either blank. Never free text. First-match-wins. Most specific value.

date_type (first match wins)
Value

commencement_date

term_end_date

extended_expiry_date

vacate_date

surrender_date

termination_date

document_date

execution_date

effective_date

other

event_role (first match wins)
Value

commencement

term_end

term_extension

early_termination

surrender

vacate

document_signed

effective

statutory

other

7. Range Patterns
Two lifecycle dates in a clear range (e.g. "from 1 January 2030 to 31 December 2040"): first → commencement_date / commencement, second → term_end_date / term_end.

Do not apply to rent, payment, review, or break ranges. Do not convert durations to dates.

8. Evidence & Citation [v3.3]
Every date must be supported by internal_evidence.

No evidence → no date record.

internal_source
internal_source must contain the document title as printed.

Use the same document title on every record originating from that document.

Do not include:

Page references

Clause references

Schedule references

Footer references

File names

Solicitor references

Evidence text

Commentary

internal_evidence
internal_evidence is a structured evidence array.

Each evidence object represents a single source reference supporting the extracted date.

Evidence Structure
Each evidence object must contain:



{
  "page": 7,
  "clause": "Particulars",
  "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
}
Field

Description

page

Page number where the evidence appears.

clause

Clause, paragraph, schedule, heading, table row, or section reference. Use an empty string if no reference is printed.

text

Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted date.

Anchor Rule
Every date record must contain at least one anchor evidence object.

The anchor evidence object must contain the printed calendar date supporting date_value.

The anchor evidence object must:

Contain the printed date.

Come from a single page.

Come from a single clause or source reference.

Be verbatim.

Be the shortest wording necessary to support the extracted date.

Support the lifecycle event being extracted.

Examples:



{
  "page": 7,
  "clause": "Particulars",
  "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
}


{
  "page": 42,
  "clause": "Schedule 5",
  "text": "The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
}
Lifecycle Purity Guard
Evidence must support a lifecycle date.

Do not include evidence that relates solely to:

Rent

Licence fees

Rent commencement dates

Rent reviews

Payment dates

Break dates

Break notice deadlines

Service charge dates

Insurance payment dates

Where a clause contains both lifecycle wording and excluded wording, quote only the lifecycle wording.

If this cannot be achieved because of OCR quality, use:



[OCR_UNREADABLE_QUOTE]
Evidence Rules
Each evidence object must:

Represent one source reference.

Be verbatim.

Be relevant to the extracted lifecycle date.

Contain only the wording necessary to support the extraction.

Each evidence object must not:

Contain commentary.

Contain reasoning.

Contain conclusions.

Contain assumptions.

Contain page references inside the text field.

Contain clause references inside the text field.

Contain multiple quotations joined together.

Contain pipe separators (|).

Multiple Supporting Sources
Where multiple source references support the same date, add additional evidence objects.

Correct:



"internal_evidence": [
  {
    "page": 7,
    "clause": "Particulars",
    "text": "Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025"
  },
  {
    "page": 42,
    "clause": "Schedule 5",
    "text": "The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
  }
]
Incorrect:



"internal_evidence": "Page 7 Particulars: Term Commencement Date: 22 August 2025 | Page 42 Schedule 5: The Term shall expire on 21 August 2035"
Emission Gate
Do not emit a record where:

internal_evidence is empty.

No evidence object contains the printed date supporting date_value.

Evidence contains commentary, reasoning, or conclusions.

Evidence contains inferred dates.

Evidence contains computed dates.

Evidence contains multiple source references in a single evidence object.

Evidence contains non-lifecycle wording only.

Evidence contains page references or clause references inside the text field.

Every emitted date record must be traceable back to at least one structured evidence object containing the printed calendar date that supports the extracted value.

9. OCR Tolerance
Condition

Action

Recoverable date (all three components determinable despite garbled characters)

Correct, set date_value, note correction.

Genuinely unrecoverable

Blank.

Date identified in notes

date_value must be populated.

10. date_description
Full operative clause text copied exactly as printed. Include provisos, conditions, alternatives, cross-references, parenthetical wording, all punctuation. No paraphrase. Single line string.

11. Deduplication and Atomic Source
Merge only if ALL match: date_value, date_type, event_role. When multiple source references support the same date record, append additional evidence objects to the internal_evidence array.

Do not combine evidence using "|" separators.

Single-Source Atomic Rule: Same date from same clause → one record only (most specific per first-match-wins). Multiple records for the same date require different clauses with different operative wording.

12. Notes
Short plain-language comment. Examples: "Contractual term start date", "Date of Agreement for Lease referenced in definitions". Do not invent dates. Do not restate clause text. Uncertain → date_type: "other", event_role: "other", explain briefly.

13. Term Object
Always returned alongside key_dates. Populate from printed term details.

Construction rules
Rule

Detail

commencement_date record created

Set term_start_date immediately.

term_end_date record created

Set term_end_date immediately.

Duration explicitly printed

term_period (e.g. "One hundred and fifty years").

is_fixed_term

true only when clearly printed.

Never calculate or infer

Never compute an end date from a period.

No term info

Empty defaults: all fields "", is_fixed_term: false.

⚠ Validity: commencement_date or term_end_date in key_dates must have matching term object field. Mismatch = invalid.

⚠ term_end_date population: Only from a printed calendar date. A duration in term_period does NOT justify computing term_end_date. If no end date is printed → term_end_date = "".

14. Pre-Output Validation
Check

Rule

date_value

ISO YYYY-MM-DD or "" — no computed dates.

date_type and event_role

Populated from vocabularies — never empty.

internal_source

Document title on every record.

internal_evidence

Must be an array.

Every evidence object contains:

page

clause

text

Evidence text must be verbatim.

Evidence text must not contain:

commentary

reasoning

conclusions

page references

clause references

joined quotations using "|"

date_description

Full operative clause verbatim.

Term matching

commencement_date and term_end_date have matching term fields.

No derived dates

No inferred, calculated, or derived dates anywhere.

No nulls

—

Mandatory checks
Check

Rule

This-Document Boundary

date_type other than "other" → must control THIS document's lifecycle. Referenced instrument date → change to "other" / "other".

No-Date-No-Record

At least one internal_evidence object must contain the printed calendar date supporting date_value.

If no evidence object contains the printed date, delete the record. If not → DELETE.

No Cross-Clause Borrowing

date_value must be printed in the cited clause. Borrowed → DELETE.

No Computed Dates

date_value derived from duration + start date → DELETE. term_end_date record without a printed end date → DELETE.

Section 5 Exclusion

Date relates solely to rent/licence fee/review/break/payment → DELETE. Notes saying "out of scope" while record exists = validation failure.

Single-Source

Same date_value + same clause + different date_types → keep most specific only.

internal_source

Must be document title on every record. Page numbers or solicitor codes → correct.

schema.title: Key Dates Extraction Schema v3.3
schema.description: Schema for extracting lifecycle dates from property lease documents. Version 3.3 updates internal_evidence from a free-text string to a structured evidence array.

key_dates.description: Array of individual lifecycle date records extracted from documents in the chain.

date_value.description: The calendar date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Never computed from a duration.

date_type.description: What kind of date this is.

event_role.description: What this date does in the tenancy or document lifecycle.

date_description.description: Verbatim operative clause text. Never paraphrased.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted date. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted date. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Any additional context or observations. Must not contain evidence text, page references, clause references, or structured field values already captured elsewhere.

term.description: Summary of the tenancy term as extracted from the document.

term_period.description: The duration of the term as printed. For example, '10 years'. Empty string if not printed.

term_start_date.description: When the term begins in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Should match the commencement_date record in key_dates where present. Never computed from a duration.

term_end_date.description: When the term is scheduled to end in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Only populated if a full calendar date is printed. Never computed from a duration.

is_fixed_term.description: Whether this is a fixed-term tenancy.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted term summary. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted term. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.description: The effective date of the document

fields.name: description
fields.description: Overall document description that will help find this document on landlord's queries.

fields.name: Consideration

schema.title: Consideration Extraction Schema [v1.4]
schema.description: Schema for extracting lease and licence consideration data from property documents. Version 1.4 separates fixed consideration, rent and licence fee model terms, and contingent consideration mechanisms such as overage, clawback, uplift and profit share.

consideration.description: Array of fixed payment obligation records extracted from the lease or licence. Use this for fixed rent, ground rent, base rent, peppercorn rent, premium, reverse premium, landlord contribution and licence fee records only.

consideration_type.description: The category of fixed payment. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

payment_amount.description: The monetary amount. 0 is permitted for peppercorn rent only. All others must be greater than 0. Landlord-to-tenant payments are recorded as positive amounts.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, for example GBP. Peppercorn rent must be empty string. No currency symbols.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before this fixed payment obligation takes effect. Empty string when unconditional.

amount_basis.description: The basis on which the amount is expressed.

payment_frequency.description: How often payment is made. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where no recurring frequency applies.

payment_timing.description: Whether payment is made in advance or arrears. Use 'not_stated' for one-off payments where neither applies.

payment_dates.description: Specific dates on which payment falls due. Free text, verbatim from the lease where printed.

payment_start_date.description: Start date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

payment_end_date.description: End date of this payment obligation in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

rent_commencement_date.description: The date rent or licence fee payments begin. ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DD, or empty string if not printed or ambiguous.

consideration_change_type.description: How this record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original record being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the fixed payment obligation.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted payment obligation. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

rent_model_terms.description: Array of occupation payment adjustment or calculation mechanism records. Use this for rent or licence fee mechanisms only. Do not use this array for overage, clawback, uplift, profit share, development gain, planning gain or other contingent consideration mechanisms.

term_type.description: Classification of the rent or licence fee mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the rent or licence fee mechanism works. Must begin with: HIGHER_OF, TOP_UP, FIXED_PLUS_VARIABLE, VALUATION_BASED, or STANDALONE.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Starting amount for arithmetic_progression, or the fixed amount printed in the source clause. Empty string for all other term types or when no fixed digits appear.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism depends on external data, such as index values or turnover figures.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs needed. Empty array if none required.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the rent or licence fee mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

contingent_consideration.description: Array of contingent, deferred, variable or event-triggered consideration payment mechanisms which are not fixed occupation rent and are not rent calculation mechanisms. Use this for overage, clawback, uplift payments, profit share, development gain, planning gain, additional consideration, further consideration and similar landlord-benefiting contingent payments.

consideration_type.description: Use only for contingent consideration obligations that are not fixed rent, premium, licence fee or landlord contribution.

mechanism.description: The specific type of contingent consideration mechanism.

mechanism_summary.description: Plain-language summary of how the contingent payment is calculated or triggered. Do not calculate the payment.

trigger_event.description: Plain-language description of the event that triggers the payment obligation. Empty string if not clear.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code or empty string if no currency is printed.

fixed_amount_printed.description: Any fixed monetary amount printed as part of the contingent mechanism. Empty string if none.

requires_external_inputs.description: Whether the mechanism requires external information to determine the amount payable, such as sale price, profit, valuation, development cost, planning uplift, lettings income or disposal proceeds.

external_inputs.description: List of external inputs required to calculate the contingent payment. Empty array if none.

condition.description: Plain-language description of any condition that must be satisfied before the contingent payment obligation applies. Empty string when unconditional.

consideration_change_type.description: How this contingent consideration record relates to the chain of documents.

targets_consideration_description.description: Plain-language description of the original contingent consideration being varied or removed. Empty string on original and addition records.

internal_source.description: Document title or filename only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the contingent consideration mechanism.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, definition, table row, or heading reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted contingent consideration mechanism. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Plain text only. No lease wording, no clause references, no evidence text, and no internal prompt terms.

fields.name: key_dates
fields.description:
[v1.1] Deed of Variation — Key Dates Extraction Prompt
 

Objective
Extract lifecycle dates that are created, amended, or referenced by this Deed of Variation.

Return strict JSON matching the Key Dates Schema.

Extract only dates that are relevant to the variation.

Do not calculate dates.

Do not derive dates from durations.

Do not create dates that are not expressly printed.

If no dates are found:



{
  "key_dates": [],
  "term": {
    "term_start_date": "",
    "term_end_date": "",
    "term_period": "",
    "is_fixed_term": false
  }
}
Core Principle
A Deed of Variation amends an existing lease.

The extraction must capture:

the date of the deed;

the effective date of the variation;

any new commencement dates;

any new expiry dates;

any term extension dates;

any new review dates;

any new break dates;

any other printed lifecycle dates introduced by the variation.

The extraction must not capture:

dates merely recited for historical background;

dates repeated solely to identify the original lease;

dates that have not changed and are restated for context.

Extract the date because it matters to the variation.

Ignore dates that exist only as recital material.

Document Date
Where the deed is dated:

Extract:



"date_type": "document_date"
and:



"event_role": "document_signed"
This will normally be the primary date in the document.

Effective Date
Where the deed states that the variation takes effect from a specific date:

Extract:



"date_type": "effective_date"
and:



"event_role": "effective"
Examples:

effective from 1 January 2026;

deemed effective from completion;

effective on the date of this deed.

Only extract printed dates.

Do not calculate completion dates.

Term Extension Dates
Where the variation extends the term:

Extract the revised expiry date.

Populate:



"date_type": "extended_expiry_date"
and:



"event_role": "term_extension"
Examples:

term extended until 24 March 2040;

expiry date changed to 31 December 2035.

Revised Term End Dates
Where the deed replaces the original expiry date:

Extract the new printed expiry date.

Populate:



"date_type": "term_end_date"
and:



"event_role": "term_end"
Use the revised date only.

Do not extract the superseded expiry date.

Revised Commencement Dates
Where the deed changes the commencement date:

Extract the new commencement date.

Populate:



"date_type": "commencement_date"
and:



"event_role": "commencement"
Use the revised date only.

Break Dates
Where the deed adds or varies a break right and a calendar date is expressly printed:

Extract:



"date_type": "other"
Use:



"event_role": "early_termination"
Only where a specific calendar date is printed.

Do not calculate break dates from anniversary wording.

Those belong in the Breaks extraction.

Rent Review Dates
Where the deed introduces or changes a review date and a calendar date is expressly printed:

Extract:



"date_type": "other"
Use:



"event_role": "other"
Only where a specific date is printed.

Do not calculate review dates from anniversary wording.

Those belong in the Consideration Review extraction.

Surrender Dates
Where the deed records a surrender taking effect on a specific date:

Extract:



"date_type": "surrender_date"
and:



"event_role": "surrender"
Term Object
Populate the term object only where the variation changes the term.

term_start_date
Populate only where a commencement date is expressly varied or stated.

term_end_date
Populate only where a revised expiry date is expressly printed.

Never calculate from duration wording.

term_period
Populate only where a revised term length is expressly printed.

Examples:



15 years


10 years less 1 day
is_fixed_term
Use:



true
only where a fixed term is expressly stated.

Otherwise:



false
Recital Rules
Many deeds restate original lease dates.

Examples:



Lease dated 25 March 2015


Term commencing 25 March 2015
These dates often appear only to identify the original lease.

Do not extract them unless the deed changes them or relies upon them as part of the variation.

Full Document Scan
Review the entire deed.

Relevant dates may appear within:

recitals;

definitions;

operative clauses;

variation provisions;

schedules;

supplemental provisions.

Do not rely solely on headings.

Evidence Rules
Every date record must contain:



"internal_source"
and:



"internal_evidence"
internal_source
Use the document title only.

internal_evidence
Use the structured evidence array required by the schema.

Each evidence object must contain:



{
  "page": 0,
  "clause": "",
  "text": ""
}
Evidence must:

be verbatim;

support the extracted date;

support the date classification;

support the variation being made.

Use the minimum number of evidence objects necessary.

Interpretation Rules
Extract only printed dates.

Never:

calculate anniversaries;

calculate expiry dates;

derive dates from durations;

infer effective dates;

infer commencement dates.

Where wording is unclear:

preserve uncertainty;

use an empty date_value if required by the schema;

do not guess.

Deed Of Variation Rules
Rule

Requirement

Document date

Usually extracted

Effective date

Extract where expressly stated

Revised commencement dates

Extract

Revised expiry dates

Extract

Term extensions

Extract

Review dates

Only if calendar date printed

Break dates

Only if calendar date printed

Historical recital dates

Ignore unless varied

Date calculations

Never perform

Validation Checklist
Before output confirm:

Check

Requirement

Dates are printed

Yes

No calculated dates

Yes

Effective dates extracted where stated

Yes

Revised term dates extracted where stated

Yes

Historical recital dates excluded

Yes

term object aligned with extracted dates

Yes

internal_source populated

Yes

internal_evidence populated

Yes

Output Rules
Return strict JSON only.

No commentary.

No explanations.

No legal analysis.

No summaries.

schema.title: Key Dates Extraction Schema v3.3
schema.description: Schema for extracting lifecycle dates from property lease documents. Version 3.3 updates internal_evidence from a free-text string to a structured evidence array.

key_dates.description: Array of individual lifecycle date records extracted from documents in the chain.

date_value.description: The calendar date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Never computed from a duration.

date_type.description: What kind of date this is.

event_role.description: What this date does in the tenancy or document lifecycle.

date_description.description: Verbatim operative clause text. Never paraphrased.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted date. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted date. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.

notes.description: Any additional context or observations. Must not contain evidence text, page references, clause references, or structured field values already captured elsewhere.

term.description: Summary of the tenancy term as extracted from the document.

term_period.description: The duration of the term as printed. For example, '10 years'. Empty string if not printed.

term_start_date.description: When the term begins in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Should match the commencement_date record in key_dates where present. Never computed from a duration.

term_end_date.description: When the term is scheduled to end in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) or empty string. Only populated if a full calendar date is printed. Never computed from a duration.

is_fixed_term.description: Whether this is a fixed-term tenancy.

internal_source.description: Document title only. No page references, clause references, schedules, evidence text, or commentary.

internal_evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the extracted term summary. Each item represents one source reference. Evidence must be verbatim and must not contain commentary, reasoning, summaries, conclusions, or extracted interpretations.

page.description: Page number where the evidence appears.

clause.description: Clause, paragraph, schedule, table row, heading, or section reference. Empty string if no reference is printed.

text.description: Short verbatim extract supporting the extracted term. No paraphrase, commentary, reasoning, conclusions, page references, or clause references.
